content.content:
1	# Story 3.4: Refactor `release.yaml` to push to `main` via auto-merge PR
2	
3	Status: review
4	
5	> **Review state note (2026-04-23).** Code refactor merged on `main` as commit [`58726dd`](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/commit/58726dd) via [PR #199](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/pull/199). Tasks 1–6 complete. Tasks 7–9 are post-merge review activities: Task 7 (AC #11 live validation) piggy-backs on Story 5.2's `v1.0.0-rc.1` RC cut; Task 8 (sprint-status `review → done` + issue #198 close) gates on Task 7; Task 9 (`/bmad-code-review` on fresh context) is a user-triggered step. Story 3.4 → `done` when Task 7 validation succeeds.
6	
7	> **Origin.** Filed 2026-04-23 as GitHub issue [#198](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/issues/198) during Story 5.2 execution. The canonical `release.yaml § Push commit to main` step (`release.yaml:235-237`) failed on run `24829166764` — branch-protection ruleset `Default` (id `13855503`) rejected the `github-actions[bot]` direct push with `remote: error: GH013: Repository rule violations found for refs/heads/main. Changes must be made through a pull request. 7 of 7 required status checks are expected.` Root cause: the ruleset's `bypass_actors` admits only the Admin `RepositoryRole` (`actor_id: 5`, `bypass_mode: pull_request`) — NOT the `github-actions[bot]` integration. Adding the global `GitHub Actions` integration (`actor_id: 15368`) as a bypass actor was rejected by GitHub with `Actor GitHub Actions integration must be part of the ruleset source or owner organization` because `armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge` is a user-owned repo (global Integration bypass is admitted only under org-level rulesets). AC 17 of Story 5.2 incorrectly assumed `bypass_mode: pull_request` covered the bot's direct push; it does not.
8	>
9	> **Impact.** Story 5.2 is paused at Task 3. Its Commit 1 (`3fc1f00`, pre-RC hand-bump to `1.0.0-rc.0`) is on `main`. The dangling remote tag `v1.0.0-rc.1` has been deleted. No npm publish occurred. Dist-tags unchanged. Epic 3 flipped `done` → `in-progress` and Story 5.2 Status marked BLOCKED pending this refactor.
10	>
11	> **Remediation chosen (Option B from issue #198).** Refactor `release.yaml` to push the release commit on a short-lived temp branch, open a PR against `main`, wait for the 7 required checks to pass + maintainer approval, then auto-merge the PR. This keeps branch protection unweakened, stays on `GITHUB_TOKEN` (no new credentials to manage), matches the native GitHub pattern used by `dependabot` / `renovate` / `semantic-release`, and re-runs CI on the release commit (catches any regression in the workflow's `Update marketplace.json version` / `Update CHANGELOG.md` / `Restore CHANGELOG preamble` steps before it hits `main`).
12	
13	<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->
14	
15	## Story
16	
17	As the maintainer,
18	I want `release.yaml` to push the `release: bump to vX.Y.Z` commit to `main` via an auto-merged bot PR (instead of a direct `git push origin HEAD:main`),
19	so that the 7 required status checks gate every release commit, branch protection remains unweakened, and releases dispatched from `main` (RC / latest / patch / minor / major) no longer fail at the push step — unblocking Story 5.2's `v1.0.0-rc.1` cut and every subsequent non-alpha release.
20	
21	## Acceptance Criteria
22	
23	1. **Temp-branch push replaces the direct `main` push (main-dispatch path).** On `workflow_dispatch` with `github.ref == refs/heads/main`, after `release.yaml § Commit version bump` creates the `release: bump to vX.Y.Z` commit in the runner's local checkout, the workflow SHALL push that commit onto a temp branch named `release/bot/v<new_version>-<run_id>` (full pattern: `release/bot/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}-${{ github.run_id }}`) on `origin`. The `github.run_id` suffix is load-bearing: a second concurrent release dispatch would collide on a `release/bot/vX.Y.Z` bare name; `concurrency: { group: release, cancel-in-progress: false }` at `release.yaml:29-31` serializes dispatches BUT a failed retry on the same version string (e.g., `1.0.0-rc.1` cut that fails at the PR stage, then re-dispatched after a fix) would re-use `release/bot/v1.0.0-rc.1` if the suffix were absent. The `run_id` is strictly monotonic per-repo, so each dispatch gets a unique branch. The push SHALL use `GITHUB_TOKEN` (already wired via `actions/checkout@v4`'s `token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}` at `release.yaml:47`) — NO new credentials. Branch creation on a non-`main` ref is NOT gated by the ruleset (ruleset `13855503` targets `refs/heads/main` per `gh api /repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/rulesets/13855503 --jq .conditions`). Verification: `git ls-remote --heads origin release/bot/v<new_version>-<run_id>` post-push SHALL return the commit SHA.
24	
25	2. **PR opened from the temp branch to `main` via `gh pr create`.** After the temp-branch push, the workflow SHALL run `gh pr create --base main --head release/bot/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}-${{ github.run_id }} --title 'release: bump to v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}' --body <body>` with the body containing: (a) a one-line description — `Automated release PR opened by release.yaml run ${{ github.run_id }}. Auto-merges once the 7 required status checks pass and a maintainer approves.`; (b) the generated release notes (from `release.yaml § Generate release notes`, available via `steps.release_notes.outputs.RELEASE_NOTES`); (c) a link back to the workflow run (`${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}`); (d) a footer line `> This PR is authored by github-actions[bot] under GITHUB_TOKEN; human approval is required to merge.`. The PR number SHALL be captured into a step output (`pr_number`) for downstream steps. The `gh` CLI is pre-installed on ubuntu-latest runners; `env: { GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} }` at the step level authenticates it. Verification: `gh pr view <pr_number> --json number,state,headRefName,baseRefName` SHALL return `{"number":<N>,"state":"OPEN","headRefName":"release/bot/v...","baseRefName":"main"}`.
26	
27	3. **Known constraint — PR opened via `GITHUB_TOKEN` may NOT trigger `quality.yaml`; the workflow MUST force-trigger checks.** Per GitHub Actions documentation (`https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/triggering-a-workflow#triggering-a-workflow-from-a-workflow`): "When you use the repository's `GITHUB_TOKEN` to perform tasks, events triggered by the `GITHUB_TOKEN`, with the exception of `workflow_dispatch` and `repository_dispatch`, will not create a new workflow run." A `pull_request` event opened by the bot therefore SHALL NOT fire `quality.yaml` (which triggers on `pull_request: branches: ["**"]` per `.github/workflows/quality.yaml:16-18`). Without those runs, the 7 required checks (`prettier`, `eslint`, `markdownlint`, `validate (ubuntu-latest)`, `validate (windows-latest)`, `python (ubuntu-latest)`, `python (windows-latest)`) remain in `expected` state forever — `gh pr checks <pr_number>` would show them missing, and the ruleset blocks merge indefinitely. **The dev agent SHALL choose and implement ONE of the following three remediation paths** (ordered by recommended preference), document the chosen path in the Dev Agent Record, and wire it into `release.yaml`:
28	
29	   - **(3.i) [RECOMMENDED] `workflow_dispatch` re-trigger of `quality.yaml` against the temp branch.** After opening the PR, `release.yaml` SHALL run `gh workflow run quality.yaml --ref release/bot/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}-${{ github.run_id }}`. The `workflow_dispatch` event is GitHub's documented exception — it IS allowed to trigger from `GITHUB_TOKEN`. Caveat to verify at dev time: `workflow_dispatch`-triggered check runs may post under a different `context` name (e.g., `Quality & Validation / prettier` instead of bare `prettier`) — if so, the 7 required-checks names on the ruleset won't match, and the PR stays blocked. Verification path: dry-run on a throwaway branch (Task 3 below) and inspect `gh pr checks` output vs. the 7 ruleset names. If the names mismatch, either (a) rename the ruleset checks (blast radius: affects every PR, owned by the ruleset definition — not this story's territory), (b) add matching job-name aliases in `quality.yaml` (scope creep into quality.yaml), or (c) fall through to (3.ii) / (3.iii). Document the outcome.
30	
31	   - **(3.ii) Close-and-reopen trick to fire the `pull_request` event under a non-`GITHUB_TOKEN` actor.** After opening the PR, `release.yaml` SHALL `gh pr close <pr_number>` then `gh pr reopen <pr_number>`. GitHub's current behavior (as of 2026-04): `gh pr reopen` when authenticated as `GITHUB_TOKEN` still counts as a `GITHUB_TOKEN`-triggered event — so this MAY NOT fire `quality.yaml` either. Kept in the option space because it's a known workaround in the ecosystem but its effectiveness on this specific repo must be verified empirically. Lower-preference; fall-back only.
32	
33	   - **(3.iii) Personal Access Token (PAT) or fine-grained GitHub App token stored as repo secret (e.g., `RELEASE_BOT_TOKEN`).** When `gh pr create` authenticates as a PAT owned by Armel (or a dedicated machine user), the PR is authored under that user account and `pull_request` events fire `quality.yaml` normally. Costs: (a) one more credential to rotate (counter to issue #198's stated rationale of "no new credentials to manage"); (b) PAT scope must be minimum-viable (contents:read+write on this repo, pull-requests:read+write, actions:read — the narrowest GitHub Apps surface is preferred over classic PATs). Highest complexity, highest reliability. Use only if (3.i) and (3.ii) both fail at dev-agent verification. Document the token's scope + expiration + rotation plan in `docs/RELEASING.md` if adopted.
34	
35	   The workflow's step that handles this SHALL be named `Force-trigger required status checks on bot PR` with a comment referencing AC #3 + issue #198 so a future reader understands WHY this step is here and not just what it does.
36	
37	4. **Workflow waits for all 7 required status checks to conclude.** After the force-trigger step at AC #3, `release.yaml` SHALL poll `gh pr checks <pr_number>` until every check in the ruleset's `required_status_checks` list reaches `conclusion in {success, failure, skipped, cancelled, timed_out, action_required}`. The poll SHALL use a simple bash loop: `while true; do ... gh pr checks ... ; done` with a `sleep 30` between iterations and a hard timeout of 20 minutes (Story 3.2's successful alpha cut ran `quality.yaml` in ~5-8 min; 20 min gives 2.5-4× headroom for matrix-runner contention). On timeout, the workflow SHALL exit non-zero with `::error::PR <pr_number> checks did not complete within 20m. PR left open for manual inspection; re-run release.yaml after investigation.` (NOT auto-close — per epic AC "if checks fail on the bot PR, the workflow should exit and leave the PR open for manual inspection — not auto-close"). On ALL checks concluding with `success`, the workflow proceeds to AC #5. On ANY check concluding with `failure` / `action_required` / `cancelled` / `timed_out`, the workflow SHALL exit non-zero with `::error::PR <pr_number> has failing check(s). PR left open for manual inspection. Re-run release.yaml after the underlying defect is fixed on main.` AND the PR SHALL NOT be merged by this run (branch protection would block it anyway, but defense-in-depth). The 7 required check names SHALL be enumerated as a bash array at the top of the wait step — hard-coded. If the ruleset's check list ever changes, this array and `release.yaml` must be updated in lockstep (drift protection via a CI-asserted diff belongs in a follow-up hardening story; out of Story 3.4 scope).
38	
39	5. **Maintainer approves the bot PR; workflow waits for approval.** After AC #4 signals all checks green, `release.yaml` SHALL poll `gh pr view <pr_number> --json reviewDecision,reviews` until `reviewDecision == APPROVED`. Poll cadence: `sleep 60` between iterations; hard timeout 30 minutes (operator-in-the-loop step; 30 min allows the maintainer time to return to the approval UI). The maintainer (Armel — `@armelhbobdad`) SHALL approve the PR via the GitHub review UI. **GitHub's universal self-approval rule applies:** a PR's author cannot approve their own PR. Because this PR is authored by `github-actions[bot]` (NOT by Armel), Armel CAN approve it — his user is a distinct actor from the bot. This is distinct from the `release` environment's `required_reviewers` gate (which fires at the `environment: release` job-level gate inside `release.yaml` itself, NOT at the PR merge gate). Both gates must be satisfied independently: (a) at the env-gate (early in the workflow run), (b) at the PR-approval gate (before PR auto-merge). Timeout handling: `::error::PR <pr_number> was not approved within 30m. PR left open. Re-run release.yaml after approval.` and exit non-zero.
40	
41	6. **Auto-merge enablement on the repo (precondition for `gh pr merge --auto`).** The repo currently has `allow_auto_merge: false` (verified at create-story time via `gh api /repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge --jq .allow_auto_merge`). Before the first release cut through the refactored workflow, the maintainer SHALL enable auto-merge on the repo either (a) via the GitHub UI (Settings → General → Pull Requests → "Allow auto-merge"), or (b) via `gh api --method PATCH /repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge -f allow_auto_merge=true`. This is a one-time setup action recorded in Dev Agent Record § Debug Log References with the timestamp + method. If auto-merge is NOT enabled at dispatch time, `gh pr merge --auto` SHALL fail with `auto merge is not allowed for this repository`; the workflow SHOULD surface this clearly as an actionable error in its step output (wrap the `gh pr merge` call in a bash `if` block that checks the exit code and emits `::error::Auto-merge is not enabled on this repository. Run: gh api --method PATCH /repos/${{ github.repository }} -f allow_auto_merge=true`). Alternative path: if the maintainer declines to enable auto-merge (policy reasons), the workflow SHALL instead poll for `mergeable_state == clean` after AC #4 + AC #5 and call plain `gh pr merge <pr_number> --merge` (blocking form) — but this requires the workflow itself to run the merge AFTER approval, which the poll at AC #5 already handles. Recommendation: enable auto-merge (simpler; matches native pattern); fall back to poll-then-plain-merge only if policy forbids.
42	
43	7. **PR auto-merge via `gh pr merge --auto --merge` (recommended) OR plain merge after explicit polling.** After AC #4 (checks green) + AC #5 (approval received), the workflow SHALL call `gh pr merge ${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_number }} --auto --merge` (NOT `--squash`, NOT `--rebase`). Rationale: **merge-commit** preserves the exact `release: bump to vX.Y.Z` commit SHA that the workflow authored — which is critical because the `Create and push tag` step creates `git tag v<new_version>` pointing at that SHA (AC #8). If the PR were squashed, the original commit SHA would be orphaned (the squash creates a new commit on `main`) and the tag would point at an orphan. Matches the existing PR #195 / #196 / #197 merge-commit pattern on this repo. Alternative (if AC #6's auto-merge path is declined): after AC #4 + AC #5 both signal green, call `gh pr merge ${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_number }} --merge` directly (no `--auto`), which performs the merge synchronously and waits for GitHub's merge API to return. Either path: verify post-merge via `gh pr view <pr_number> --json merged,mergedAt,mergeCommit`; `merged == true` and `mergeCommit.oid != null`.
44	
45	8. **Tag creation moves to AFTER PR merge (ordering change).** The current `release.yaml § Create and push tag` step (lines 226-233) pushes `v<new_version>` to origin BEFORE `Push commit to main` (lines 235-237) and BEFORE the npm publish step (lines 246-255). After Story 3.4's refactor: the tag step SHALL run AFTER the PR merge completes (AC #7) and BEFORE the npm publish step. Rationale: the tag must point at a commit that is actually on `main` — if the tag is pushed before the PR merges (as today), it points at the orphaned temp-branch commit which IS on the temp branch but NOT yet on `main`. After the PR auto-merges, the bot PR's commit becomes the new `main` tip (merge-commit path preserves the SHA per AC #7); the tag pushed now points at a `main`-reachable commit. New ordering in `release.yaml`: `Commit version bump` → **[NEW]** `Push to temp branch` → `Open PR` → `Force-trigger checks` → `Wait for checks` → `Wait for approval` → `Auto-merge PR` → **[MOVED]** `Create and push tag` → `Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing` → `Create GitHub Release` → `Summary`. **Dependency on temp-branch commit SHA:** after the PR auto-merges via `--merge` (merge-commit), the bot's commit SHA on `main` IS the same SHA that was on the temp branch (merge-commit creates a NEW merge commit but PRESERVES the original commits underneath). The tag creation step SHALL capture this via: `git fetch origin main && NEW_COMMIT_SHA=$(git rev-parse origin/main) && git tag -a v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" $NEW_COMMIT_SHA` — this anchors the tag on the merged bot commit (the parent of the merge commit), NOT on the merge commit itself. OR alternatively: anchor the tag on the merge commit directly (simpler; matches what `gh release` expects; the compare URL Generate-release-notes step at `release.yaml:217` is already "from previous_tag to HEAD" which would work either way). Dev agent decides: **recommend the merge-commit** as the tag anchor (simpler, and future `git describe --tags` from `main` returns the tag cleanly). Commit that decision into the workflow + this story's Dev Notes at implementation time.
46	
47	9. **Non-main dispatch path (alpha-cut from feature branch) preserved UNCHANGED.** When `github.ref != refs/heads/main` (the Story 3.2 alpha-cut pattern: dispatch from `feat/release-workflow` or equivalent), the workflow SHALL skip the entire PR dance (AC #1 through AC #7) and retain the current `release.yaml:239-244` behavior verbatim: `::notice::Dispatched from <ref> (not main); skipping 'git push origin HEAD:main'.` + `Tag v<version> was pushed; main was NOT advanced.`. The tag still gets created on the orphaned CI-ephemeral commit (as today — the alpha at `0.10.1-alpha.0` created an orphan at `2a57dcbd` per Epic 3 retro; this is NFR12-compliant and the expected pattern for feature-branch prereleases). Concrete step-level conditionals: every new PR-related step (AC #1 through AC #7) SHALL carry `if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'`. The existing "Skip main push" step stays as-is with `if: github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'`. **Verification:** after the refactor lands, a dry-run dispatch from `feat/release-workflow` with `version_bump: alpha` SHALL behave identically to Story 3.2's alpha cut (same steps fire, same tag-only push, same npm publish, same orphan-commit pattern). This is tested at Task 7 (alpha-cut validation) below.
48	
49	10. **Scope envelope — all release channels (alpha/beta/rc/patch/minor/major) exercise the same PR path from `main`.** Per issue #198 proposed AC 10: any `main`-dispatched cut — whether the `version_bump` input is `alpha` (`1.0.0-alpha.0`), `beta`, `rc`, `patch`, `minor`, or `major` — SHALL traverse the same AC #1–#8 PR flow. The non-main-dispatch alpha path (AC #9) remains the alternative for feature-branch prereleases that intentionally don't touch `main`. This means the `if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'` conditional is the ONLY branch — there is NO secondary "use PR for some version types, direct push for others" logic. Simpler, fewer code paths, fewer failure modes. **Dry-run verification candidate:** the alpha cut at Task 7 SHALL validate that an alpha cut DISPATCHED FROM MAIN (not feature branch) exercises the PR path and produces `1.0.0-alpha.N` on npm with the `alpha` dist-tag — exercising the channel + PR-path crossproduct. An alpha cut from feature branch (AC #9 path) is a separate validation that the legacy non-main path still works.
50	
51	11. **E2E validation — cut an RC or alpha through the refactored workflow.** After the refactor lands on `main` (Story 3.4 PR merged), the workflow MUST be validated end-to-end before Story 5.2 resumes. Two acceptable validation paths:
52	
53	    - **(11.i) [RECOMMENDED] Cut `v1.0.0-rc.1` via the refactored workflow (Story 5.2 resumption IS the validator).** After Story 3.4 closes, Story 5.2 dispatches `release.yaml -f version_bump=rc --ref main` from `main` tip. This is the native validation — Story 5.2's AC 6–AC 8 (provenance, dist-tag, side-effects) become the ground truth for "the refactor works." Story 5.2 dev agent will need to refresh AC 17 (remove the now-invalid "bypass covers the bot push" language) and AC 4 (the dispatch still uses `--ref main` but the `Push commit to main` step is replaced by the PR flow). Flag for Story 5.2 re-sequencing: the Story 5.2 dev agent SHALL re-read Story 5.2 ACs in light of the new flow and propose AC drift fixes at Task 1 recon (before re-dispatch).
54	
55	    - **(11.ii) Alpha cut from `main` BEFORE Story 5.2 resumes.** Dispatch `release.yaml -f version_bump=alpha --ref main` to publish an alpha version (e.g., `0.10.1-alpha.1`) under `--tag alpha` as a throwaway dress-rehearsal of the new PR flow. This exercises the full AC #1–AC #10 path, produces a real npm artifact (verifying no regression in the marketplace.json / CHANGELOG / OIDC publish chain), and leaves the `rc` cut for Story 5.2. Blast radius is low (alpha versions are discardable; `latest` unchanged). Marginal npm version-number consumption (burns one alpha slot). Recommended only if the dev agent is less confident about the refactor and wants a lower-stakes validation before the RC cycle — adds a tag + run + GitHub Release + npm publish, which IS some churn. **Choose (11.i) unless a specific reason requires (11.ii).**
56	
57	    Whichever path is chosen, the validation SHALL produce: (a) a successful workflow run with `conclusion: success`; (b) an npm publish with provenance attestation; (c) a tag on `main` pointing at the merged bot commit; (d) a bot PR merged via auto-merge with all 7 checks green; (e) `main` advanced by exactly one merge commit + the bot's commit underneath. Record all artifacts (run URL, PR URL, tag URL, npm URL) in Dev Agent Record.
58	
59	12. **`docs/RELEASING.md § Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` prose refresh.** The existing cutting procedure at `docs/RELEASING.md:559-592` (authored in Story 5.1) describes a release flow that assumes direct push to `main`. After Story 3.4 lands: Story 3.4 SHALL NOT edit this section (scope: workflow code, not docs). Instead, Story 3.4 SHALL: (a) add a new `### Story 3.4 refactor note` sub-section at the bottom of `docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher` or at the bottom of the `Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` block — 3-5 lines describing the PR-auto-merge flow ("release.yaml opens a bot PR rather than pushing direct; approval required at both the `release` environment gate AND the PR review UI"); (b) flag the need for a Story 5.2-internal prose refresh (AC 17 + AC 4 of Story 5.2). The broader rewrite of `§ Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` prose to match the new flow is Story 5.2's dev-agent territory (when it resumes from Task 3). **Alternative consideration:** if the prose delta is small enough (a few lines), Story 3.4 MAY fold it in — but only if the edit stays tightly scoped to the mechanical flow description, not the surrounding rationale. Recommendation: prose-refresh in Story 5.2; note-only in Story 3.4. Dev agent chooses at implementation time; document in Dev Notes.
60	
61	13. **CODEOWNERS / code-owner-review semantics (unchanged).** The ruleset has `require_code_owner_review: true` per `rulesets/13855503 --jq '.rules[] | select(.type=="pull_request") | .parameters.require_code_owner_review'`, BUT no `.github/CODEOWNERS` file exists in the repo (verified at create-story time: `ls .github/` returns `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`, `FUNDING.yaml`, `ISSUE_TEMPLATE/`, `scripts/`, `workflows/` — no `CODEOWNERS`). GitHub treats the code-owner requirement as vacuously satisfied when the file is absent (per `docs/RELEASING.md:40`). Story 3.4 SHALL NOT create a CODEOWNERS file — that is explicitly out of scope. If a future story creates one (post-Story-3.4), the author must verify that `github-actions[bot]`-opened PRs from `release/bot/...` branches do NOT trigger a code-owner gate that the bot cannot satisfy. Flag for the author of any future CODEOWNERS addition: scope the CODEOWNERS patterns to NOT match `release/bot/*` temp branches, OR explicitly list the maintainer as the code-owner and have the maintainer approve the bot PR (same pattern as AC #5). Outside Story 3.4's territory; captured here so a future reader doesn't double-back.
62	
63	14. **Concurrency — concurrent release dispatches (future scenario).** Today releases are serialized manually by cadence (one maintainer, one release at a time). The existing `concurrency: { group: release, cancel-in-progress: false }` at `release.yaml:29-31` ensures at most one `release.yaml` run is active at a time. Combined with AC #1's temp-branch-name-includes-`${{ github.run_id }}` pattern, two simultaneous dispatches (which `cancel-in-progress: false` causes to queue, not kill) cannot collide on a branch name. Scope: Story 3.4 inherits the existing concurrency block unchanged. A future two-parallel-releases scenario (e.g., an org with two maintainers shipping independent packages from one repo — not currently relevant to SKF) would need re-examination of this block. Flag for post-Story-3.4 awareness; no action this story.
64	
65	15. **Workflow output structure — step outputs + GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY.** `release.yaml § Summary` step (lines 266-274) SHALL be extended to include: (a) the bot PR URL (`https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/pull/<pr_number>`); (b) a new subheading `### Release Flow` that enumerates the flow taken — `Temp branch: release/bot/vX.Y.Z-<run_id>` → `PR #<N> opened` → `Checks passed: <list>` → `Approved by @<user> at <timestamp>` → `Auto-merged at <timestamp>` → `Tag vX.Y.Z pushed at <merge-commit-SHA>` → `npm publish completed`. This serves as the operator's single-page run summary. FR20 (`$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` emission — spec line 130) remains satisfied. No new functional behavior; summary-polish only.
66	
67	16. **Defect-response protocol if the refactor surfaces an issue at AC #11 validation.** If the validation cut (AC #11) fails at any point:
68	    - **(a) Temp-branch push fails** (AC #1) — ruleset regression (unlikely) or permission regression; verify `GITHUB_TOKEN` scope at `permissions: { contents: write }` (release.yaml:26) — present today; if absent, restore.
69	    - **(b) PR creation fails** (AC #2) — most common cause: `gh` CLI's `GH_TOKEN` env var not set. Ensure every `gh` command in the new steps has `env: { GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} }` at the step level (or at the job level once, which is cleaner).
70	    - **(c) Checks don't fire on the PR** (AC #3 critical risk) — fall through the (3.i) → (3.ii) → (3.iii) preference chain. Record which path was required; flag future-story consideration of (3.iii) adoption.
71	    - **(d) Check-wait times out** (AC #4) — investigate `gh pr checks` output, decide whether the cause is a slow runner (extend timeout) or a genuinely stuck check (surface the specific failing check).
72	    - **(e) Approval never arrives** (AC #5) — maintainer forgot to approve; manual remediation, no workflow-code fix.
73	    - **(f) Auto-merge fails despite all checks + approval green** (AC #6 / AC #7) — investigate `gh pr view --json mergeable,mergeStateStatus` — likely cause: `mergeable_state in {behind, dirty, unstable, blocked}`. Document + add remediation step.
74	    - **(g) Tag points at wrong commit** (AC #8 ordering bug) — the tag anchor SHA capture logic after merge is the most subtle part; verify the tag via `git log <tag>^..HEAD` after cut.
75	    - **(h) Non-main alpha cut regresses** (AC #9) — dispatch `release.yaml -f version_bump=alpha --ref feat/<any-branch>` and confirm the old path still fires; if broken, the `if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'` conditionals were mis-placed.
76	    Each failure class SHALL be captured in Dev Agent Record § Completion Notes with the remediation applied.
77	
78	17. **Rollback path if Story 3.4 itself ships a broken refactor.** If the refactored `release.yaml` is merged to `main` and the first real cut (Story 5.2's RC) fails in a way the dev agent can't immediately fix, the rollback is: (a) `gh pr revert <Story-3.4-PR-number>` creates a revert PR; (b) approve + merge the revert PR; (c) `release.yaml` returns to the pre-3.4 direct-`main`-push form — which the issue #198 root cause (ruleset rejection of bot push) still blocks, so Story 5.2's AC 17 blocker returns. The revert is ONLY useful if Story 3.4 introduces a NEW defect that's worse than the pre-3.4 state. Most likely scenario: revert is never used; fix-forward via a Story 3.4.1 sub-story or a code-review patch on Story 3.4 itself. Flag: the revert path is a contingency, not a planned exit.
79	
80	18. **Evidence artifact in Dev Agent Record.** The dev agent SHALL record:

file.content:
# Story 3.4: Refactor `release.yaml` to push to `main` via auto-merge PR

Status: review

> **Review state note (2026-04-23).** Code refactor merged on `main` as commit [`58726dd`](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/commit/58726dd) via [PR #199](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/pull/199). Tasks 1–6 complete. Tasks 7–9 are post-merge review activities: Task 7 (AC #11 live validation) piggy-backs on Story 5.2's `v1.0.0-rc.1` RC cut; Task 8 (sprint-status `review → done` + issue #198 close) gates on Task 7; Task 9 (`/bmad-code-review` on fresh context) is a user-triggered step. Story 3.4 → `done` when Task 7 validation succeeds.

> **Origin.** Filed 2026-04-23 as GitHub issue [#198](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/issues/198) during Story 5.2 execution. The canonical `release.yaml § Push commit to main` step (`release.yaml:235-237`) failed on run `24829166764` — branch-protection ruleset `Default` (id `13855503`) rejected the `github-actions[bot]` direct push with `remote: error: GH013: Repository rule violations found for refs/heads/main. Changes must be made through a pull request. 7 of 7 required status checks are expected.` Root cause: the ruleset's `bypass_actors` admits only the Admin `RepositoryRole` (`actor_id: 5`, `bypass_mode: pull_request`) — NOT the `github-actions[bot]` integration. Adding the global `GitHub Actions` integration (`actor_id: 15368`) as a bypass actor was rejected by GitHub with `Actor GitHub Actions integration must be part of the ruleset source or owner organization` because `armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge` is a user-owned repo (global Integration bypass is admitted only under org-level rulesets). AC 17 of Story 5.2 incorrectly assumed `bypass_mode: pull_request` covered the bot's direct push; it does not.
>
> **Impact.** Story 5.2 is paused at Task 3. Its Commit 1 (`3fc1f00`, pre-RC hand-bump to `1.0.0-rc.0`) is on `main`. The dangling remote tag `v1.0.0-rc.1` has been deleted. No npm publish occurred. Dist-tags unchanged. Epic 3 flipped `done` → `in-progress` and Story 5.2 Status marked BLOCKED pending this refactor.
>
> **Remediation chosen (Option B from issue #198).** Refactor `release.yaml` to push the release commit on a short-lived temp branch, open a PR against `main`, wait for the 7 required checks to pass + maintainer approval, then auto-merge the PR. This keeps branch protection unweakened, stays on `GITHUB_TOKEN` (no new credentials to manage), matches the native GitHub pattern used by `dependabot` / `renovate` / `semantic-release`, and re-runs CI on the release commit (catches any regression in the workflow's `Update marketplace.json version` / `Update CHANGELOG.md` / `Restore CHANGELOG preamble` steps before it hits `main`).

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As the maintainer,
I want `release.yaml` to push the `release: bump to vX.Y.Z` commit to `main` via an auto-merged bot PR (instead of a direct `git push origin HEAD:main`),
so that the 7 required status checks gate every release commit, branch protection remains unweakened, and releases dispatched from `main` (RC / latest / patch / minor / major) no longer fail at the push step — unblocking Story 5.2's `v1.0.0-rc.1` cut and every subsequent non-alpha release.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Temp-branch push replaces the direct `main` push (main-dispatch path).** On `workflow_dispatch` with `github.ref == refs/heads/main`, after `release.yaml § Commit version bump` creates the `release: bump to vX.Y.Z` commit in the runner's local checkout, the workflow SHALL push that commit onto a temp branch named `release/bot/v<new_version>-<run_id>` (full pattern: `release/bot/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}-${{ github.run_id }}`) on `origin`. The `github.run_id` suffix is load-bearing: a second concurrent release dispatch would collide on a `release/bot/vX.Y.Z` bare name; `concurrency: { group: release, cancel-in-progress: false }` at `release.yaml:29-31` serializes dispatches BUT a failed retry on the same version string (e.g., `1.0.0-rc.1` cut that fails at the PR stage, then re-dispatched after a fix) would re-use `release/bot/v1.0.0-rc.1` if the suffix were absent. The `run_id` is strictly monotonic per-repo, so each dispatch gets a unique branch. The push SHALL use `GITHUB_TOKEN` (already wired via `actions/checkout@v4`'s `token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}` at `release.yaml:47`) — NO new credentials. Branch creation on a non-`main` ref is NOT gated by the ruleset (ruleset `13855503` targets `refs/heads/main` per `gh api /repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/rulesets/13855503 --jq .conditions`). Verification: `git ls-remote --heads origin release/bot/v<new_version>-<run_id>` post-push SHALL return the commit SHA.

2. **PR opened from the temp branch to `main` via `gh pr create`.** After the temp-branch push, the workflow SHALL run `gh pr create --base main --head release/bot/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}-${{ github.run_id }} --title 'release: bump to v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}' --body <body>` with the body containing: (a) a one-line description — `Automated release PR opened by release.yaml run ${{ github.run_id }}. Auto-merges once the 7 required status checks pass and a maintainer approves.`; (b) the generated release notes (from `release.yaml § Generate release notes`, available via `steps.release_notes.outputs.RELEASE_NOTES`); (c) a link back to the workflow run (`${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}`); (d) a footer line `> This PR is authored by github-actions[bot] under GITHUB_TOKEN; human approval is required to merge.`. The PR number SHALL be captured into a step output (`pr_number`) for downstream steps. The `gh` CLI is pre-installed on ubuntu-latest runners; `env: { GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} }` at the step level authenticates it. Verification: `gh pr view <pr_number> --json number,state,headRefName,baseRefName` SHALL return `{"number":<N>,"state":"OPEN","headRefName":"release/bot/v...","baseRefName":"main"}`.

3. **Known constraint — PR opened via `GITHUB_TOKEN` may NOT trigger `quality.yaml`; the workflow MUST force-trigger checks.** Per GitHub Actions documentation (`https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/triggering-a-workflow#triggering-a-workflow-from-a-workflow`): "When you use the repository's `GITHUB_TOKEN` to perform tasks, events triggered by the `GITHUB_TOKEN`, with the exception of `workflow_dispatch` and `repository_dispatch`, will not create a new workflow run." A `pull_request` event opened by the bot therefore SHALL NOT fire `quality.yaml` (which triggers on `pull_request: branches: ["**"]` per `.github/workflows/quality.yaml:16-18`). Without those runs, the 7 required checks (`prettier`, `eslint`, `markdownlint`, `validate (ubuntu-latest)`, `validate (windows-latest)`, `python (ubuntu-latest)`, `python (windows-latest)`) remain in `expected` state forever — `gh pr checks <pr_number>` would show them missing, and the ruleset blocks merge indefinitely. **The dev agent SHALL choose and implement ONE of the following three remediation paths** (ordered by recommended preference), document the chosen path in the Dev Agent Record, and wire it into `release.yaml`:

   - **(3.i) [RECOMMENDED] `workflow_dispatch` re-trigger of `quality.yaml` against the temp branch.** After opening the PR, `release.yaml` SHALL run `gh workflow run quality.yaml --ref release/bot/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}-${{ github.run_id }}`. The `workflow_dispatch` event is GitHub's documented exception — it IS allowed to trigger from `GITHUB_TOKEN`. Caveat to verify at dev time: `workflow_dispatch`-triggered check runs may post under a different `context` name (e.g., `Quality & Validation / prettier` instead of bare `prettier`) — if so, the 7 required-checks names on the ruleset won't match, and the PR stays blocked. Verification path: dry-run on a throwaway branch (Task 3 below) and inspect `gh pr checks` output vs. the 7 ruleset names. If the names mismatch, either (a) rename the ruleset checks (blast radius: affects every PR, owned by the ruleset definition — not this story's territory), (b) add matching job-name aliases in `quality.yaml` (scope creep into quality.yaml), or (c) fall through to (3.ii) / (3.iii). Document the outcome.

   - **(3.ii) Close-and-reopen trick to fire the `pull_request` event under a non-`GITHUB_TOKEN` actor.** After opening the PR, `release.yaml` SHALL `gh pr close <pr_number>` then `gh pr reopen <pr_number>`. GitHub's current behavior (as of 2026-04): `gh pr reopen` when authenticated as `GITHUB_TOKEN` still counts as a `GITHUB_TOKEN`-triggered event — so this MAY NOT fire `quality.yaml` either. Kept in the option space because it's a known workaround in the ecosystem but its effectiveness on this specific repo must be verified empirically. Lower-preference; fall-back only.

   - **(3.iii) Personal Access Token (PAT) or fine-grained GitHub App token stored as repo secret (e.g., `RELEASE_BOT_TOKEN`).** When `gh pr create` authenticates as a PAT owned by Armel (or a dedicated machine user), the PR is authored under that user account and `pull_request` events fire `quality.yaml` normally. Costs: (a) one more credential to rotate (counter to issue #198's stated rationale of "no new credentials to manage"); (b) PAT scope must be minimum-viable (contents:read+write on this repo, pull-requests:read+write, actions:read — the narrowest GitHub Apps surface is preferred over classic PATs). Highest complexity, highest reliability. Use only if (3.i) and (3.ii) both fail at dev-agent verification. Document the token's scope + expiration + rotation plan in `docs/RELEASING.md` if adopted.

   The workflow's step that handles this SHALL be named `Force-trigger required status checks on bot PR` with a comment referencing AC #3 + issue #198 so a future reader understands WHY this step is here and not just what it does.

4. **Workflow waits for all 7 required status checks to conclude.** After the force-trigger step at AC #3, `release.yaml` SHALL poll `gh pr checks <pr_number>` until every check in the ruleset's `required_status_checks` list reaches `conclusion in {success, failure, skipped, cancelled, timed_out, action_required}`. The poll SHALL use a simple bash loop: `while true; do ... gh pr checks ... ; done` with a `sleep 30` between iterations and a hard timeout of 20 minutes (Story 3.2's successful alpha cut ran `quality.yaml` in ~5-8 min; 20 min gives 2.5-4× headroom for matrix-runner contention). On timeout, the workflow SHALL exit non-zero with `::error::PR <pr_number> checks did not complete within 20m. PR left open for manual inspection; re-run release.yaml after investigation.` (NOT auto-close — per epic AC "if checks fail on the bot PR, the workflow should exit and leave the PR open for manual inspection — not auto-close"). On ALL checks concluding with `success`, the workflow proceeds to AC #5. On ANY check concluding with `failure` / `action_required` / `cancelled` / `timed_out`, the workflow SHALL exit non-zero with `::error::PR <pr_number> has failing check(s). PR left open for manual inspection. Re-run release.yaml after the underlying defect is fixed on main.` AND the PR SHALL NOT be merged by this run (branch protection would block it anyway, but defense-in-depth). The 7 required check names SHALL be enumerated as a bash array at the top of the wait step — hard-coded. If the ruleset's check list ever changes, this array and `release.yaml` must be updated in lockstep (drift protection via a CI-asserted diff belongs in a follow-up hardening story; out of Story 3.4 scope).

5. **Maintainer approves the bot PR; workflow waits for approval.** After AC #4 signals all checks green, `release.yaml` SHALL poll `gh pr view <pr_number> --json reviewDecision,reviews` until `reviewDecision == APPROVED`. Poll cadence: `sleep 60` between iterations; hard timeout 30 minutes (operator-in-the-loop step; 30 min allows the maintainer time to return to the approval UI). The maintainer (Armel — `@armelhbobdad`) SHALL approve the PR via the GitHub review UI. **GitHub's universal self-approval rule applies:** a PR's author cannot approve their own PR. Because this PR is authored by `github-actions[bot]` (NOT by Armel), Armel CAN approve it — his user is a distinct actor from the bot. This is distinct from the `release` environment's `required_reviewers` gate (which fires at the `environment: release` job-level gate inside `release.yaml` itself, NOT at the PR merge gate). Both gates must be satisfied independently: (a) at the env-gate (early in the workflow run), (b) at the PR-approval gate (before PR auto-merge). Timeout handling: `::error::PR <pr_number> was not approved within 30m. PR left open. Re-run release.yaml after approval.` and exit non-zero.

6. **Auto-merge enablement on the repo (precondition for `gh pr merge --auto`).** The repo currently has `allow_auto_merge: false` (verified at create-story time via `gh api /repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge --jq .allow_auto_merge`). Before the first release cut through the refactored workflow, the maintainer SHALL enable auto-merge on the repo either (a) via the GitHub UI (Settings → General → Pull Requests → "Allow auto-merge"), or (b) via `gh api --method PATCH /repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge -f allow_auto_merge=true`. This is a one-time setup action recorded in Dev Agent Record § Debug Log References with the timestamp + method. If auto-merge is NOT enabled at dispatch time, `gh pr merge --auto` SHALL fail with `auto merge is not allowed for this repository`; the workflow SHOULD surface this clearly as an actionable error in its step output (wrap the `gh pr merge` call in a bash `if` block that checks the exit code and emits `::error::Auto-merge is not enabled on this repository. Run: gh api --method PATCH /repos/${{ github.repository }} -f allow_auto_merge=true`). Alternative path: if the maintainer declines to enable auto-merge (policy reasons), the workflow SHALL instead poll for `mergeable_state == clean` after AC #4 + AC #5 and call plain `gh pr merge <pr_number> --merge` (blocking form) — but this requires the workflow itself to run the merge AFTER approval, which the poll at AC #5 already handles. Recommendation: enable auto-merge (simpler; matches native pattern); fall back to poll-then-plain-merge only if policy forbids.

7. **PR auto-merge via `gh pr merge --auto --merge` (recommended) OR plain merge after explicit polling.** After AC #4 (checks green) + AC #5 (approval received), the workflow SHALL call `gh pr merge ${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_number }} --auto --merge` (NOT `--squash`, NOT `--rebase`). Rationale: **merge-commit** preserves the exact `release: bump to vX.Y.Z` commit SHA that the workflow authored — which is critical because the `Create and push tag` step creates `git tag v<new_version>` pointing at that SHA (AC #8). If the PR were squashed, the original commit SHA would be orphaned (the squash creates a new commit on `main`) and the tag would point at an orphan. Matches the existing PR #195 / #196 / #197 merge-commit pattern on this repo. Alternative (if AC #6's auto-merge path is declined): after AC #4 + AC #5 both signal green, call `gh pr merge ${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_number }} --merge` directly (no `--auto`), which performs the merge synchronously and waits for GitHub's merge API to return. Either path: verify post-merge via `gh pr view <pr_number> --json merged,mergedAt,mergeCommit`; `merged == true` and `mergeCommit.oid != null`.

8. **Tag creation moves to AFTER PR merge (ordering change).** The current `release.yaml § Create and push tag` step (lines 226-233) pushes `v<new_version>` to origin BEFORE `Push commit to main` (lines 235-237) and BEFORE the npm publish step (lines 246-255). After Story 3.4's refactor: the tag step SHALL run AFTER the PR merge completes (AC #7) and BEFORE the npm publish step. Rationale: the tag must point at a commit that is actually on `main` — if the tag is pushed before the PR merges (as today), it points at the orphaned temp-branch commit which IS on the temp branch but NOT yet on `main`. After the PR auto-merges, the bot PR's commit becomes the new `main` tip (merge-commit path preserves the SHA per AC #7); the tag pushed now points at a `main`-reachable commit. New ordering in `release.yaml`: `Commit version bump` → **[NEW]** `Push to temp branch` → `Open PR` → `Force-trigger checks` → `Wait for checks` → `Wait for approval` → `Auto-merge PR` → **[MOVED]** `Create and push tag` → `Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing` → `Create GitHub Release` → `Summary`. **Dependency on temp-branch commit SHA:** after the PR auto-merges via `--merge` (merge-commit), the bot's commit SHA on `main` IS the same SHA that was on the temp branch (merge-commit creates a NEW merge commit but PRESERVES the original commits underneath). The tag creation step SHALL capture this via: `git fetch origin main && NEW_COMMIT_SHA=$(git rev-parse origin/main) && git tag -a v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" $NEW_COMMIT_SHA` — this anchors the tag on the merged bot commit (the parent of the merge commit), NOT on the merge commit itself. OR alternatively: anchor the tag on the merge commit directly (simpler; matches what `gh release` expects; the compare URL Generate-release-notes step at `release.yaml:217` is already "from previous_tag to HEAD" which would work either way). Dev agent decides: **recommend the merge-commit** as the tag anchor (simpler, and future `git describe --tags` from `main` returns the tag cleanly). Commit that decision into the workflow + this story's Dev Notes at implementation time.

9. **Non-main dispatch path (alpha-cut from feature branch) preserved UNCHANGED.** When `github.ref != refs/heads/main` (the Story 3.2 alpha-cut pattern: dispatch from `feat/release-workflow` or equivalent), the workflow SHALL skip the entire PR dance (AC #1 through AC #7) and retain the current `release.yaml:239-244` behavior verbatim: `::notice::Dispatched from <ref> (not main); skipping 'git push origin HEAD:main'.` + `Tag v<version> was pushed; main was NOT advanced.`. The tag still gets created on the orphaned CI-ephemeral commit (as today — the alpha at `0.10.1-alpha.0` created an orphan at `2a57dcbd` per Epic 3 retro; this is NFR12-compliant and the expected pattern for feature-branch prereleases). Concrete step-level conditionals: every new PR-related step (AC #1 through AC #7) SHALL carry `if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'`. The existing "Skip main push" step stays as-is with `if: github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'`. **Verification:** after the refactor lands, a dry-run dispatch from `feat/release-workflow` with `version_bump: alpha` SHALL behave identically to Story 3.2's alpha cut (same steps fire, same tag-only push, same npm publish, same orphan-commit pattern). This is tested at Task 7 (alpha-cut validation) below.

10. **Scope envelope — all release channels (alpha/beta/rc/patch/minor/major) exercise the same PR path from `main`.** Per issue #198 proposed AC 10: any `main`-dispatched cut — whether the `version_bump` input is `alpha` (`1.0.0-alpha.0`), `beta`, `rc`, `patch`, `minor`, or `major` — SHALL traverse the same AC #1–#8 PR flow. The non-main-dispatch alpha path (AC #9) remains the alternative for feature-branch prereleases that intentionally don't touch `main`. This means the `if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'` conditional is the ONLY branch — there is NO secondary "use PR for some version types, direct push for others" logic. Simpler, fewer code paths, fewer failure modes. **Dry-run verification candidate:** the alpha cut at Task 7 SHALL validate that an alpha cut DISPATCHED FROM MAIN (not feature branch) exercises the PR path and produces `1.0.0-alpha.N` on npm with the `alpha` dist-tag — exercising the channel + PR-path crossproduct. An alpha cut from feature branch (AC #9 path) is a separate validation that the legacy non-main path still works.

11. **E2E validation — cut an RC or alpha through the refactored workflow.** After the refactor lands on `main` (Story 3.4 PR merged), the workflow MUST be validated end-to-end before Story 5.2 resumes. Two acceptable validation paths:

    - **(11.i) [RECOMMENDED] Cut `v1.0.0-rc.1` via the refactored workflow (Story 5.2 resumption IS the validator).** After Story 3.4 closes, Story 5.2 dispatches `release.yaml -f version_bump=rc --ref main` from `main` tip. This is the native validation — Story 5.2's AC 6–AC 8 (provenance, dist-tag, side-effects) become the ground truth for "the refactor works." Story 5.2 dev agent will need to refresh AC 17 (remove the now-invalid "bypass covers the bot push" language) and AC 4 (the dispatch still uses `--ref main` but the `Push commit to main` step is replaced by the PR flow). Flag for Story 5.2 re-sequencing: the Story 5.2 dev agent SHALL re-read Story 5.2 ACs in light of the new flow and propose AC drift fixes at Task 1 recon (before re-dispatch).

    - **(11.ii) Alpha cut from `main` BEFORE Story 5.2 resumes.** Dispatch `release.yaml -f version_bump=alpha --ref main` to publish an alpha version (e.g., `0.10.1-alpha.1`) under `--tag alpha` as a throwaway dress-rehearsal of the new PR flow. This exercises the full AC #1–AC #10 path, produces a real npm artifact (verifying no regression in the marketplace.json / CHANGELOG / OIDC publish chain), and leaves the `rc` cut for Story 5.2. Blast radius is low (alpha versions are discardable; `latest` unchanged). Marginal npm version-number consumption (burns one alpha slot). Recommended only if the dev agent is less confident about the refactor and wants a lower-stakes validation before the RC cycle — adds a tag + run + GitHub Release + npm publish, which IS some churn. **Choose (11.i) unless a specific reason requires (11.ii).**

    Whichever path is chosen, the validation SHALL produce: (a) a successful workflow run with `conclusion: success`; (b) an npm publish with provenance attestation; (c) a tag on `main` pointing at the merged bot commit; (d) a bot PR merged via auto-merge with all 7 checks green; (e) `main` advanced by exactly one merge commit + the bot's commit underneath. Record all artifacts (run URL, PR URL, tag URL, npm URL) in Dev Agent Record.

12. **`docs/RELEASING.md § Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` prose refresh.** The existing cutting procedure at `docs/RELEASING.md:559-592` (authored in Story 5.1) describes a release flow that assumes direct push to `main`. After Story 3.4 lands: Story 3.4 SHALL NOT edit this section (scope: workflow code, not docs). Instead, Story 3.4 SHALL: (a) add a new `### Story 3.4 refactor note` sub-section at the bottom of `docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher` or at the bottom of the `Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` block — 3-5 lines describing the PR-auto-merge flow ("release.yaml opens a bot PR rather than pushing direct; approval required at both the `release` environment gate AND the PR review UI"); (b) flag the need for a Story 5.2-internal prose refresh (AC 17 + AC 4 of Story 5.2). The broader rewrite of `§ Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` prose to match the new flow is Story 5.2's dev-agent territory (when it resumes from Task 3). **Alternative consideration:** if the prose delta is small enough (a few lines), Story 3.4 MAY fold it in — but only if the edit stays tightly scoped to the mechanical flow description, not the surrounding rationale. Recommendation: prose-refresh in Story 5.2; note-only in Story 3.4. Dev agent chooses at implementation time; document in Dev Notes.

13. **CODEOWNERS / code-owner-review semantics (unchanged).** The ruleset has `require_code_owner_review: true` per `rulesets/13855503 --jq '.rules[] | select(.type=="pull_request") | .parameters.require_code_owner_review'`, BUT no `.github/CODEOWNERS` file exists in the repo (verified at create-story time: `ls .github/` returns `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`, `FUNDING.yaml`, `ISSUE_TEMPLATE/`, `scripts/`, `workflows/` — no `CODEOWNERS`). GitHub treats the code-owner requirement as vacuously satisfied when the file is absent (per `docs/RELEASING.md:40`). Story 3.4 SHALL NOT create a CODEOWNERS file — that is explicitly out of scope. If a future story creates one (post-Story-3.4), the author must verify that `github-actions[bot]`-opened PRs from `release/bot/...` branches do NOT trigger a code-owner gate that the bot cannot satisfy. Flag for the author of any future CODEOWNERS addition: scope the CODEOWNERS patterns to NOT match `release/bot/*` temp branches, OR explicitly list the maintainer as the code-owner and have the maintainer approve the bot PR (same pattern as AC #5). Outside Story 3.4's territory; captured here so a future reader doesn't double-back.

14. **Concurrency — concurrent release dispatches (future scenario).** Today releases are serialized manually by cadence (one maintainer, one release at a time). The existing `concurrency: { group: release, cancel-in-progress: false }` at `release.yaml:29-31` ensures at most one `release.yaml` run is active at a time. Combined with AC #1's temp-branch-name-includes-`${{ github.run_id }}` pattern, two simultaneous dispatches (which `cancel-in-progress: false` causes to queue, not kill) cannot collide on a branch name. Scope: Story 3.4 inherits the existing concurrency block unchanged. A future two-parallel-releases scenario (e.g., an org with two maintainers shipping independent packages from one repo — not currently relevant to SKF) would need re-examination of this block. Flag for post-Story-3.4 awareness; no action this story.

15. **Workflow output structure — step outputs + GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY.** `release.yaml § Summary` step (lines 266-274) SHALL be extended to include: (a) the bot PR URL (`https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/pull/<pr_number>`); (b) a new subheading `### Release Flow` that enumerates the flow taken — `Temp branch: release/bot/vX.Y.Z-<run_id>` → `PR #<N> opened` → `Checks passed: <list>` → `Approved by @<user> at <timestamp>` → `Auto-merged at <timestamp>` → `Tag vX.Y.Z pushed at <merge-commit-SHA>` → `npm publish completed`. This serves as the operator's single-page run summary. FR20 (`$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` emission — spec line 130) remains satisfied. No new functional behavior; summary-polish only.

16. **Defect-response protocol if the refactor surfaces an issue at AC #11 validation.** If the validation cut (AC #11) fails at any point:
    - **(a) Temp-branch push fails** (AC #1) — ruleset regression (unlikely) or permission regression; verify `GITHUB_TOKEN` scope at `permissions: { contents: write }` (release.yaml:26) — present today; if absent, restore.
    - **(b) PR creation fails** (AC #2) — most common cause: `gh` CLI's `GH_TOKEN` env var not set. Ensure every `gh` command in the new steps has `env: { GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} }` at the step level (or at the job level once, which is cleaner).
    - **(c) Checks don't fire on the PR** (AC #3 critical risk) — fall through the (3.i) → (3.ii) → (3.iii) preference chain. Record which path was required; flag future-story consideration of (3.iii) adoption.
    - **(d) Check-wait times out** (AC #4) — investigate `gh pr checks` output, decide whether the cause is a slow runner (extend timeout) or a genuinely stuck check (surface the specific failing check).
    - **(e) Approval never arrives** (AC #5) — maintainer forgot to approve; manual remediation, no workflow-code fix.
    - **(f) Auto-merge fails despite all checks + approval green** (AC #6 / AC #7) — investigate `gh pr view --json mergeable,mergeStateStatus` — likely cause: `mergeable_state in {behind, dirty, unstable, blocked}`. Document + add remediation step.
    - **(g) Tag points at wrong commit** (AC #8 ordering bug) — the tag anchor SHA capture logic after merge is the most subtle part; verify the tag via `git log <tag>^..HEAD` after cut.
    - **(h) Non-main alpha cut regresses** (AC #9) — dispatch `release.yaml -f version_bump=alpha --ref feat/<any-branch>` and confirm the old path still fires; if broken, the `if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'` conditionals were mis-placed.
    Each failure class SHALL be captured in Dev Agent Record § Completion Notes with the remediation applied.

17. **Rollback path if Story 3.4 itself ships a broken refactor.** If the refactored `release.yaml` is merged to `main` and the first real cut (Story 5.2's RC) fails in a way the dev agent can't immediately fix, the rollback is: (a) `gh pr revert <Story-3.4-PR-number>` creates a revert PR; (b) approve + merge the revert PR; (c) `release.yaml` returns to the pre-3.4 direct-`main`-push form — which the issue #198 root cause (ruleset rejection of bot push) still blocks, so Story 5.2's AC 17 blocker returns. The revert is ONLY useful if Story 3.4 introduces a NEW defect that's worse than the pre-3.4 state. Most likely scenario: revert is never used; fix-forward via a Story 3.4.1 sub-story or a code-review patch on Story 3.4 itself. Flag: the revert path is a contingency, not a planned exit.

18. **Evidence artifact in Dev Agent Record.** The dev agent SHALL record:

# Step 2: Review

## RULES

- YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
- The Blind Hunter subagent receives NO project context — diff only.
- The Edge Case Hunter subagent receives diff and project read access.
- The Acceptance Auditor subagent receives diff, spec, and context docs.

## INSTRUCTIONS

1. If `{review_mode}` = `"no-spec"`, note to the user: "Acceptance Auditor skipped — no spec file provided."

2. Launch parallel subagents without conversation context. If subagents are not available, generate prompt files in `{implementation_artifacts}` — one per reviewer role below — and HALT. Ask the user to run each in a separate session (ideally a different LLM) and paste back the findings. When findings are pasted, resume from this point and proceed to step 3.

   - **Blind Hunter** — receives `{diff_output}` only. No spec, no context docs, no project access. Invoke via the `bmad-review-adversarial-general` skill.

   - **Edge Case Hunter** — receives `{diff_output}` and read access to the project. Invoke via the `bmad-review-edge-case-hunter` skill.

   - **Acceptance Auditor** (only if `{review_mode}` = `"full"`) — receives `{diff_output}`, the content of the file at `{spec_file}`, and any loaded context docs. Its prompt:
     > You are an Acceptance Auditor. Review this diff against the spec and context docs. Check for: violations of acceptance criteria, deviations from spec intent, missing implementation of specified behavior, contradictions between spec constraints and actual code. Output findings as a Markdown list. Each finding: one-line title, which AC/constraint it violates, and evidence from the diff.

3. **Subagent failure handling**: If any subagent fails, times out, or returns empty results, append the layer name to `{failed_layers}` (comma-separated) and proceed with findings from the remaining layers.

4. Collect all findings from the completed layers.


## NEXT

Read fully and follow `./step-03-triage.md`

302	+
303	+#### Story 3.4 refactor note — PR-auto-merge flow
304	+
305	+After Story 3.4 (GitHub issue [#198](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/issues/198), PR TBD), `release.yaml` no longer pushes the `release: bump to vX.Y.Z` commit directly to `main`. Instead, a main-dispatched cut pushes the release commit to a temp branch `release/bot/vX.Y.Z-<run_id>`, opens a bot PR against `main`, force-triggers `quality.yaml` against the temp branch via `workflow_dispatch` (so the 7 required status checks run and gate the merge), then auto-merges once checks pass and a maintainer approves. Story 5.2's dev agent on resume will refresh the § Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1 prose above to describe the refactored flow directly.

+            echo "Waiting (reviewDecision: ${DECISION:-none}; merged: $MERGED; elapsed ${ELAPSED}s/${TIMEOUT_SECONDS}s)"
+            sleep $POLL_INTERVAL
+            ELAPSED=$((ELAPSED + POLL_INTERVAL))
+          done
+          echo "::error::PR #$PR_NUMBER was neither approved nor admin-bypass-merged within 30m. PR left open. Re-run release.yaml after approving or merging."
+          exit 1
+
+      - name: Auto-merge bot PR
+        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && steps.wait_approval.outputs.already_merged != 'true'
+        # AC #7: --merge (merge-commit), NOT --squash / --rebase. Merge-commit
+        # preserves the bot's release commit SHA underneath main's new tip so
+        # AC #8's tag anchor on `origin/main` resolves to a reachable commit
+        # (the merge commit itself — see the tag step below). --auto blocks
+        # nothing because checks + approval are already green.
+        # Skipped if the maintainer bypass-merged at the approval step.
+        run: |
+          PR_NUMBER="${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_number }}"
+          if ! gh pr merge "$PR_NUMBER" --auto --merge; then
+            echo "::error::Auto-merge failed for PR #$PR_NUMBER. If the error was 'auto merge is not allowed for this repository', run: gh api --method PATCH /repos/${{ github.repository }} -f allow_auto_merge=true  (see Story 3.4 AC #6 / Task 2)."
+            exit 1
+          fi
+          echo "::notice::Auto-merge enabled on PR #$PR_NUMBER"
 
-      - name: Skip main push (non-main dispatch ref)
+      - name: Wait for merge completion
+        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && steps.wait_approval.outputs.already_merged != 'true'
+        timeout-minutes: 5
+        # `gh pr merge --auto` schedules the merge; GitHub may take seconds to
+        # actually perform it. Poll `merged` until true so the downstream tag
+        # step sees the new main tip. Skipped if the maintainer bypass-merged
+        # (merged is already true).
+        run: |
+          PR_NUMBER="${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_number }}"
+          while true; do
+            MERGED=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json merged --jq .merged)
+            if [ "$MERGED" = "true" ]; then
+              echo "::notice::PR #$PR_NUMBER merged"
+              break
+            fi
+            echo "Waiting for PR merge to complete (merged: $MERGED)"
+            sleep 10
+          done
+
+      - name: Skip PR flow (non-main dispatch ref)
         if: github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'
+        # AC #9: feature-branch prereleases preserve the legacy tag-only pattern.
+        # The tag will anchor on the CI-ephemeral commit below (orphan; NFR12-
+        # compliant per the Story 3.2 alpha-cut precedent at v0.10.1-alpha.0).
         run: |
-          echo "::notice::Dispatched from ${{ github.ref }} (not main); skipping 'git push origin HEAD:main'."
-          echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} was pushed; main was NOT advanced."
+          echo "::notice::Dispatched from ${{ github.ref }} (not main); skipping PR auto-merge flow."
+          echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} will anchor on the CI-ephemeral commit; main was NOT advanced."
           echo "This is the expected path for prerelease cuts from feature branches (Story 3.2 alpha-cut pattern)."
 
+      - name: Create and push tag
+        # AC #8: tag creation moved to AFTER the PR auto-merges (main-dispatch
+        # path). The anchor is `origin/main`'s new tip (the merge commit), so
+        # `git describe --tags` from main resolves cleanly. For non-main dispatch
+        # (AC #9), anchor on HEAD (the CI-ephemeral release commit — unchanged
+        # from pre-3.4 behavior).
+        run: |
+          if [ "${{ github.ref }}" = "refs/heads/main" ]; then
+            git fetch origin main
+            TAG_ANCHOR=$(git rev-parse origin/main)
+            echo "Main-dispatch: anchoring tag on origin/main tip $TAG_ANCHOR"
+          else
+            TAG_ANCHOR=HEAD
+            echo "Non-main-dispatch: anchoring tag on CI HEAD (orphan pattern per AC #9)"
+          fi
+          if git rev-parse "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+            echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} already exists, skipping tag creation"
+          else
+            git tag -a "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" "$TAG_ANCHOR"
+            git push origin "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
+          fi
+
       - name: Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing
         env:
           NPM_TOKEN: ""
@@ -264,6 +472,9 @@ jobs:
           prerelease: ${{ contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'alpha') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'beta') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'rc') }}
 
       - name: Summary
+        # AC #15: extend the step summary with a "Release Flow" subsection so the
+        # operator sees at a glance whether the main-dispatch PR-auto-merge path
+        # or the non-main-dispatch tag-only path ran.
         run: |
           {
             echo "## 🎉 Released v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
@@ -271,4 +482,18 @@ jobs:

             echo "**GitHub**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/releases/tag/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
             echo "**Provenance**: auto-attached via OIDC trusted publishing"
+            echo
+            echo "### Release Flow"
+            echo
+            if [ "${{ github.ref }}" = "refs/heads/main" ]; then
+              echo "- Dispatch ref: \`main\` (Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge path)"
+              echo "- Temp branch: \`${{ steps.temp_push.outputs.temp_branch }}\`"
+              echo "- Bot PR: ${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_url }}"
+              echo "- Required checks: passed (7 contexts)"
+              echo "- PR approved + auto-merged via \`--merge\`"
+              echo "- Tag anchored on \`origin/main\` tip post-merge"
+            else
+              echo "- Dispatch ref: \`${{ github.ref }}\` (non-main; tag-only path per AC #9)"
+              echo "- Tag anchored on CI-ephemeral commit (orphan pattern)"
+            fi

diff --git a/docs/RELEASING.md b/docs/RELEASING.md
index c174a50..ca309a5 100644
--- a/docs/RELEASING.md
+++ b/docs/RELEASING.md
@@ -610,3 +610,7 @@ For the `release` environment, a deletion+restore similarly uses the two-call pa
   grep -c '^## \[1.0.0-rc.1\]' CHANGELOG.md   # expected: 1
   grep -c '^## \[1.0.0\] - TBD' CHANGELOG.md  # expected: 1
   ```
+
+#### Story 3.4 refactor note — PR-auto-merge flow
+
+After Story 3.4 (GitHub issue [#198](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/issues/198), PR TBD), `release.yaml` no longer pushes the `release: bump to vX.Y.Z` commit directly to `main`. Instead, a main-dispatched cut pushes the release commit to a temp branch `release/bot/vX.Y.Z-<run_id>`, opens a bot PR against `main`, force-triggers `quality.yaml` against the temp branch via `workflow_dispatch` (so the 7 required status checks run and gate the merge), then auto-merges once checks pass and a maintainer approves. Approval is required at **two** gates — the `release` environment gate at job start, and the PR review-decision gate before auto-merge. Non-main dispatches (feature-branch alpha cuts) skip the PR dance and keep the legacy tag-only behavior. Story 5.2's dev agent on resume will refresh the § Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1 prose above to describe the refactored flow directly.

You are the **Blind Hunter** adversarial code reviewer. You have NO project context — you have ONLY the diff I'm about to give you. **Do NOT read any project files, spec files, or documentation. Do NOT run git commands. Do NOT explore the repo.** Work solely from the diff.

Invoke the `bmad-review-adversarial-general` skill to perform your cynical adversarial review of this diff. If that skill is not invocable, perform the review anyway using its principles: be cynical, assume the author is wrong, hunt for bugs, security issues, logic flaws, race conditions, error-handling gaps, shell quoting / injection hazards, and anything that would break in a realistic production scenario.

The diff is a GitHub Actions workflow refactor (`.github/workflows/release.yaml`) plus a small doc addition. It replaces a direct `git push HEAD:main` with a "push to temp branch → open bot PR → force-trigger quality checks → wait for approval or admin-bypass merge → auto-merge → wait for merge → tag on origin/main tip" flow. The diff is at `/tmp/skf-3-4-diff.patch` — you may read THAT single file and nothing else.

Output findings as a Markdown list. Each finding: one-line title, severity (CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW), evidence from the diff (quote the exact lines or cite the hunk), and why it matters. Be blunt. Do not pad with confirmations of things that look fine.

Report in under 800 words.

You are the **Edge Case Hunter** code reviewer. You have the diff AND read access to the project. Your job is to walk every branching path and boundary condition in the diff and report only unhandled edge cases. Orthogonal to adversarial review — method-driven not attitude-driven.

Invoke the `bmad-review-edge-case-hunter` skill to perform the review. If the skill is not invocable, perform the review using its principles: exhaustive path enumeration, boundary conditions, concurrency interleavings, error propagation, state machine transitions, resource exhaustion, partial failures.

**Context:** The diff refactors `.github/workflows/release.yaml` (Story 3.4, issue #198). Old behavior: direct `git push HEAD:main` (rejected by branch protection ruleset). New behavior: push to temp branch `release/bot/vX.Y.Z-<run_id>` → `gh pr create` → `gh workflow run quality.yaml --ref <temp>` to force-trigger checks → poll `gh pr checks --required --watch --fail-fast` → poll for reviewDecision==APPROVED OR merged==true (admin-bypass) → `gh pr merge --auto --merge` → wait for merge → tag on `origin/main` tip.

The diff is at `/tmp/skf-3-4-diff.patch`. The project root is `/home/armel/Projects/OSS/bmad-module-skill-forge`. Read the diff first. Then feel free to read:
- `.github/workflows/release.yaml` (post-change, for context around the modified region)
- `.github/workflows/quality.yaml` (to see what the force-triggered workflow looks like)
- Any other workflow or config files that help you understand the interaction surface

Focus areas (non-exhaustive):
- Concurrency race conditions (concurrent dispatches, overlapping runs, github.run_id uniqueness assumptions)
- Shell quoting / variable expansion edge cases in the run: blocks
- Partial-failure states: temp branch pushed but PR creation fails; PR opened but force-trigger fails; checks pass but approval times out; auto-merge scheduled but merge-completion poll times out
- Timeout interactions (step-level timeout-minutes vs manual loop timers)
- State leakage across retries on the same version string
- Check-name mismatch (quality.yaml's job names vs ruleset's 7 required contexts) — does the `--required` flag correctly filter?
- What happens when `gh pr view --json reviewDecision` returns null / empty / unexpected values
- Fetch / rev-parse ordering assumptions in the tag step
- Interaction with non-main dispatch path (any steps missing `if:` guards?)

Output findings as a Markdown list. Each finding: one-line title, severity (HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW), the specific path or boundary that's unhandled, evidence (cite the diff lines), and the realistic scenario that would trigger it. Skip hypothetical scenarios that require simultaneously impossible preconditions.

Report in under 1000 words.

You are the **Acceptance Auditor** code reviewer. You have the diff AND the story spec with its acceptance criteria. Your job is to check the diff against the spec: AC violations, deviations from spec intent, missing implementation of specified behavior, contradictions between spec constraints and actual code.

**Inputs:**
- Diff: `/tmp/skf-3-4-diff.patch` (PR #199, Story 3.4)
- Spec: `/home/armel/Projects/OSS/bmad-module-skill-forge/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-4-release-workflow-push-to-main-via-pr-auto-merge.md`

**Context:** Story 3.4 refactors `release.yaml` to push to `main` via an auto-merge PR instead of direct push, to route around a branch-protection ruleset rejection (issue #198). The spec has 18 Acceptance Criteria. The story status is `review`, PR merged as commit `58726dd` on 2026-04-23.

Read the full spec (not just the summary). Read the full diff. Then walk through EACH of the 18 ACs and verify the implementation:
- AC fully implemented? ✓
- AC partially implemented? (specify the gap)
- AC missing entirely? (cite the missing behavior)
- AC deliberately deviated from? (the spec allows dev-agent choice at several points — check that the choice is reasonable AND documented)
- AC contradicted by the implementation? (red flag)

Pay particular attention to:
- AC #1 (temp branch naming includes `run_id`)
- AC #2 (PR body content — does it include all four required elements?)
- AC #3 (the force-trigger remediation path chosen — is it (3.i), (3.ii), or (3.iii)? Check the check-name-mismatch caveat is handled)
- AC #4 (check poll hard timeout 20m, 7-check enumeration)
- AC #5 (approval poll 30m timeout, self-approval rule)
- AC #6 (auto-merge enabled precondition — is this documented/handled?)
- AC #7 (--merge vs --squash vs --rebase)
- AC #8 (tag-after-merge ordering, tag anchor SHA capture)
- AC #9 (non-main dispatch preserved unchanged)
- AC #10 (all release channels traverse same PR path)
- AC #15 (GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY extensions)
- AC #16 (defect-response protocol — spec says document each failure class; is this done?)
- AC #18 (Dev Agent Record evidence — check if this was populated)

Also flag: the two commits in PR #199 are `3e915e7` (initial refactor) and `c9ccffc` (fix: accept admin-bypass merge as equivalent to approval). The second commit's behavior (admin-bypass path in the `Wait for PR approval or admin-bypass merge` step) is NOT in the original spec — that's a deviation. Check if it is a reasonable interpretation of AC #5 or a genuine scope expansion. The spec permits "plain merge after explicit polling" as an AC #7 alternative — determine if this fix commit fits that alternative or is something new.

Output findings as a Markdown table with columns: `AC #` | `Status` (✓ / partial / missing / deviated / contradicted) | `Evidence` | `Note`. After the table, a short "Spec ↔ Code gap summary" (under 200 words) listing the TOP 3 gaps that need addressing.

Report in under 1500 words.
