content.text:
D2 resolved. Moving to D3.

---

### D3 of 3 — Force-trigger check-name mismatch risk pending live validation

This is the load-bearing empirical unknown in Story 3.4. The impl chose AC #3 path (3.i) — `gh workflow run quality.yaml --ref <temp_branch>`. The open question: will the 7 posted check-runs use the bare names the ruleset expects (`prettier`, `eslint`, `markdownlint`, `validate (ubuntu-latest)`, `validate (windows-latest)`, `python (ubuntu-latest)`, `python (windows-latest)`), or will `workflow_dispatch` post them under a namespaced form (`Quality & Validation / prettier`) that the ruleset's `--required` list won't match?

Spec AC #3 enumerates the fallback chain: if names mismatch, either (a) rename ruleset checks, (b) add job-name aliases to `quality.yaml`, or (c) fall through to path (3.ii) close/reopen or (3.iii) PAT. None of this is knowable without a real dispatch.

Story 5.2's RC cut IS the validator per AC #11. Two ways to handle this:

**How would you like to resolve this?**
1. **Accept the pending-validation risk** — document it explicitly in `#### AC #3 path-3.i empirical verification — DEFERRED` (already exists in Dev Agent Record § Completion Notes); Story 5.2 dev agent handles fallback if names mismatch. Nothing to patch now.
2. **Preemptively add check-run name verification** — after `gh workflow run quality.yaml`, run `gh api repos/:owner/:repo/commits/<head_sha>/check-runs --jq '.check_runs[].name'` and assert at least one matches an expected ruleset context name. Fails fast with a specific error instead of the generic 20m timeout. Converts to a `patch` on `release.yaml`.
3. **Defer with stronger guardrails** — accept pending-validation risk AND pre-register a follow-up hardening story in sprint-status as `backlog` (one-line reason)
4. **Dismiss** — documentation in Completion Notes is sufficient; Story 5.2's validation run handles it either way

My recommendation: **Option 1**. The `gh pr checks --watch` 20m timeout already fails the run if checks don't register properly — the generic error message is imperfect but actionable enough for a solo maintainer. Option 2 is a reasonable add but it overlaps with P4 (force-trigger failure handling) and P5 (check-run count assertion) in the patch list below; those patches cover 80% of Option 2's value without duplicating the logic here. Once P4 and P5 land, most of this risk is already mitigated.

Reply with the number.

content.content:
225	            echo "npx bmad-module-skill-forge install"
226	            echo '```'
227	            echo
228	            echo "**Full Changelog**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/compare/${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}...v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
229	          } > release_notes.md
230	
231	          {
232	            echo "RELEASE_NOTES<<EOF"
233	            cat release_notes.md
234	            echo "EOF"
235	          } >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
236	
237	      # -----------------------------------------------------------------
238	      # Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge flow (main-dispatch only) — issue #198.
239	      # -----------------------------------------------------------------
240	      # Why this exists: ruleset 13855503 rejects `github-actions[bot]`'s direct
241	      # push to `main` (`GH013: Repository rule violations`). The `bypass_actors`
242	      # list admits only the Admin RepositoryRole with `bypass_mode: pull_request`,
243	      # and the repo is user-owned (not org-owned) so a global "GitHub Actions"
244	      # integration bypass cannot be added. The only routes that don't weaken
245	      # branch protection are (a) open an auto-merging PR, or (b) land a repo
246	      # transfer + org-level ruleset. We chose (a) — Option B in issue #198.
247	      #
248	      # Non-main-dispatch path (feature-branch alpha cut) stays unchanged: it
249	      # skips this whole block and takes the `Skip PR flow` step further down.
250	      # -----------------------------------------------------------------
251	
252	      - name: Push commit to temp branch
253	        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
254	        id: temp_push
255	        # AC #1: main-dispatch must NOT push directly to main. Push the release
256	        # commit onto a throwaway branch whose name includes `github.run_id` so
257	        # a retry after a mid-flow failure on the same version string cannot
258	        # collide with a prior branch (concurrency group serializes dispatches
259	        # but same-version re-dispatch after cleanup is a real scenario).
260	        run: |
261	          TEMP_BRANCH="release/bot/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}-${{ github.run_id }}"
262	          git push origin "HEAD:refs/heads/$TEMP_BRANCH"
263	          echo "temp_branch=$TEMP_BRANCH" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
264	          echo "::notice::Pushed release commit to temp branch $TEMP_BRANCH"
265	
266	      - name: Open bot PR
267	        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
268	        id: open_pr
269	        # AC #2: open a PR from the temp branch to main so branch protection's
270	        # 7 required checks gate the release commit. `--body-file` (not --body)
271	        # avoids shell-escape hazards on multi-line release notes that may
272	        # contain backticks / dollar signs / nested code fences.
273	        run: |
274	          TEMP_BRANCH="${{ steps.temp_push.outputs.temp_branch }}"
275	          NEW_VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
276	          RUN_URL="${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}"
277	
278	          {
279	            echo "Automated release PR opened by \`release.yaml\` run \`${{ github.run_id }}\`. Auto-merges once the 7 required status checks pass and a maintainer approves."
280	            echo
281	            echo "## Release notes"
282	            echo
283	            cat release_notes.md
284	            echo
285	            echo "## Workflow run"
286	            echo
287	            echo "$RUN_URL"
288	            echo
289	            echo "---"
290	            echo
291	            echo "> This PR is authored by \`github-actions[bot]\` under \`GITHUB_TOKEN\`; human approval is required to merge. See GitHub issue #198 for the refactor rationale."
292	          } > pr_body.md
293	
294	          PR_URL=$(gh pr create \
295	            --base main \
296	            --head "$TEMP_BRANCH" \
297	            --title "release: bump to v$NEW_VERSION" \
298	            --body-file pr_body.md)
299	          PR_NUMBER=$(gh pr view "$PR_URL" --json number --jq .number)
300	          echo "pr_url=$PR_URL" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
301	          echo "pr_number=$PR_NUMBER" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
302	          echo "::notice::Opened bot PR #$PR_NUMBER at $PR_URL"
303	
304	      - name: Force-trigger required status checks on bot PR
305	        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
306	        # AC #3 path 3.i — PR events authored by GITHUB_TOKEN do NOT fire
307	        # `pull_request`-triggered workflows (GitHub docs: "events triggered
308	        # by the GITHUB_TOKEN ... will not create a new workflow run",
309	        # excepting workflow_dispatch and repository_dispatch). Without this
310	        # step, the 7 required contexts would stay in "expected" state forever
311	        # and branch protection would block merge indefinitely.
312	        #
313	        # Workaround: `workflow_dispatch` is an allowed exception — dispatch
314	        # quality.yaml against the temp branch so it runs there and posts its
315	        # check-runs on the PR's head commit. If the posted check-run names do
316	        # NOT match the ruleset's 7 required contexts (e.g., namespaced to
317	        # `Quality & Validation / prettier`), fall back to path 3.ii (close-
318	        # reopen) or 3.iii (PAT) in a follow-up patch; issue #198 enumerates
319	        # all three options.
320	        run: |
321	          TEMP_BRANCH="${{ steps.temp_push.outputs.temp_branch }}"
322	          gh workflow run quality.yaml --ref "$TEMP_BRANCH"
323	          echo "::notice::Dispatched quality.yaml against $TEMP_BRANCH via workflow_dispatch"
324	
325	      - name: Wait for required status checks
326	        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
327	        timeout-minutes: 20
328	        # AC #4: poll until all required contexts conclude, 20m hard cap.
329	        # `--required` filters to only ruleset-required checks (ignores optional
330	        # runs); `--watch` polls every 10s; `--fail-fast` exits 1 on first
331	        # failure. Pre-sleep lets the workflow_dispatch from the prior step
332	        # register its check-runs on the PR's head SHA before we start watching.
333	        run: |
334	          PR_NUMBER="${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_number }}"
335	          sleep 15
336	          if ! gh pr checks "$PR_NUMBER" --required --watch --fail-fast; then
337	            echo "::error::PR #$PR_NUMBER has failing required check(s). PR left open for manual inspection. Re-run release.yaml after the underlying defect is fixed on main."
338	            exit 1
339	          fi
340	          echo "::notice::All required status checks passed on PR #$PR_NUMBER"
341	
342	      - name: Wait for PR approval or admin-bypass merge
343	        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
344	        id: wait_approval
345	        # AC #5 (robust): poll until EITHER reviewDecision == APPROVED (the bot
346	        # PR was approved, auto-merge will fire next) OR merged == true (the
347	        # maintainer bypass-merged via admin). github-actions[bot] cannot
348	        # self-approve (universal GitHub rule); `armelhbobdad` is the required
349	        # approver per the `release` env's required_reviewers list.
350	        #
351	        # The merged-true branch handles the scenario where the maintainer
352	        # chooses to bypass-merge via admin instead of approving via UI (same
353	        # pattern used on PRs #195/#196/#197 in this repo, where ruleset
354	        # 13855503's bypass_mode: pull_request admits admin merges without a
355	        # formal review). In that case, `reviewDecision` stays REVIEW_REQUIRED
356	        # forever and a pure APPROVED-only poll would hang for 30m.
357	        #
358	        # Manual timer (not step-level timeout-minutes) so we can emit a
359	        # specific ::error:: message on timeout. Exports already_merged so the
360	        # next two steps skip their work when the maintainer pre-merged.
361	        run: |
362	          PR_NUMBER="${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_number }}"
363	          TIMEOUT_SECONDS=1800  # 30 min
364	          POLL_INTERVAL=60
365	          ELAPSED=0
366	          while [ $ELAPSED -lt $TIMEOUT_SECONDS ]; do
367	            VIEW=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json reviewDecision,merged)
368	            DECISION=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r .reviewDecision)
369	            MERGED=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r .merged)
370	            if [ "$MERGED" = "true" ]; then
371	              echo "::notice::PR #$PR_NUMBER already merged (admin-bypass path); skipping auto-merge step."
372	              echo "already_merged=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
373	              exit 0
374	            fi
375	            if [ "$DECISION" = "APPROVED" ]; then
376	              echo "::notice::PR #$PR_NUMBER approved; proceeding to auto-merge."
377	              echo "already_merged=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
378	              exit 0
379	            fi
380	            echo "Waiting (reviewDecision: ${DECISION:-none}; merged: $MERGED; elapsed ${ELAPSED}s/${TIMEOUT_SECONDS}s)"
381	            sleep $POLL_INTERVAL
382	            ELAPSED=$((ELAPSED + POLL_INTERVAL))
383	          done
384	          echo "::error::PR #$PR_NUMBER was neither approved nor admin-bypass-merged within 30m. PR left open. Re-run release.yaml after approving or merging."
385	          exit 1
386	
387	      - name: Auto-merge bot PR
388	        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && steps.wait_approval.outputs.already_merged != 'true'
389	        # AC #7: --merge (merge-commit), NOT --squash / --rebase. Merge-commit
390	        # preserves the bot's release commit SHA underneath main's new tip so
391	        # AC #8's tag anchor on `origin/main` resolves to a reachable commit
392	        # (the merge commit itself — see the tag step below). --auto blocks
393	        # nothing because checks + approval are already green.
394	        # Skipped if the maintainer bypass-merged at the approval step.
395	        run: |

file.content:
echo "npx bmad-module-skill-forge install"
            echo '```'
            echo
            echo "**Full Changelog**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/compare/${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}...v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          } > release_notes.md

          {
            echo "RELEASE_NOTES<<EOF"
            cat release_notes.md
            echo "EOF"
          } >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      # -----------------------------------------------------------------
      # Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge flow (main-dispatch only) — issue #198.
      # -----------------------------------------------------------------
      # Why this exists: ruleset 13855503 rejects `github-actions[bot]`'s direct
      # push to `main` (`GH013: Repository rule violations`). The `bypass_actors`
      # list admits only the Admin RepositoryRole with `bypass_mode: pull_request`,
      # and the repo is user-owned (not org-owned) so a global "GitHub Actions"
      # integration bypass cannot be added. The only routes that don't weaken
      # branch protection are (a) open an auto-merging PR, or (b) land a repo
      # transfer + org-level ruleset. We chose (a) — Option B in issue #198.
      #
      # Non-main-dispatch path (feature-branch alpha cut) stays unchanged: it
      # skips this whole block and takes the `Skip PR flow` step further down.
      # -----------------------------------------------------------------

      - name: Push commit to temp branch
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        id: temp_push
        # AC #1: main-dispatch must NOT push directly to main. Push the release
        # commit onto a throwaway branch whose name includes `github.run_id` so
        # a retry after a mid-flow failure on the same version string cannot
        # collide with a prior branch (concurrency group serializes dispatches
        # but same-version re-dispatch after cleanup is a real scenario).
        run: |
          TEMP_BRANCH="release/bot/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}-${{ github.run_id }}"
          git push origin "HEAD:refs/heads/$TEMP_BRANCH"
          echo "temp_branch=$TEMP_BRANCH" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "::notice::Pushed release commit to temp branch $TEMP_BRANCH"

      - name: Open bot PR
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        id: open_pr
        # AC #2: open a PR from the temp branch to main so branch protection's
        # 7 required checks gate the release commit. `--body-file` (not --body)
        # avoids shell-escape hazards on multi-line release notes that may
        # contain backticks / dollar signs / nested code fences.
        run: |
          TEMP_BRANCH="${{ steps.temp_push.outputs.temp_branch }}"
          NEW_VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          RUN_URL="${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}"

          {
            echo "Automated release PR opened by \`release.yaml\` run \`${{ github.run_id }}\`. Auto-merges once the 7 required status checks pass and a maintainer approves."
            echo
            echo "## Release notes"
            echo
            cat release_notes.md
            echo
            echo "## Workflow run"
            echo
            echo "$RUN_URL"
            echo
            echo "---"
            echo
            echo "> This PR is authored by \`github-actions[bot]\` under \`GITHUB_TOKEN\`; human approval is required to merge. See GitHub issue #198 for the refactor rationale."
          } > pr_body.md

          PR_URL=$(gh pr create \
            --base main \
            --head "$TEMP_BRANCH" \
            --title "release: bump to v$NEW_VERSION" \
            --body-file pr_body.md)
          PR_NUMBER=$(gh pr view "$PR_URL" --json number --jq .number)
          echo "pr_url=$PR_URL" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "pr_number=$PR_NUMBER" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "::notice::Opened bot PR #$PR_NUMBER at $PR_URL"

      - name: Force-trigger required status checks on bot PR
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        # AC #3 path 3.i — PR events authored by GITHUB_TOKEN do NOT fire
        # `pull_request`-triggered workflows (GitHub docs: "events triggered
        # by the GITHUB_TOKEN ... will not create a new workflow run",
        # excepting workflow_dispatch and repository_dispatch). Without this
        # step, the 7 required contexts would stay in "expected" state forever
        # and branch protection would block merge indefinitely.
        #
        # Workaround: `workflow_dispatch` is an allowed exception — dispatch
        # quality.yaml against the temp branch so it runs there and posts its
        # check-runs on the PR's head commit. If the posted check-run names do
        # NOT match the ruleset's 7 required contexts (e.g., namespaced to
        # `Quality & Validation / prettier`), fall back to path 3.ii (close-
        # reopen) or 3.iii (PAT) in a follow-up patch; issue #198 enumerates
        # all three options.
        run: |
          TEMP_BRANCH="${{ steps.temp_push.outputs.temp_branch }}"
          gh workflow run quality.yaml --ref "$TEMP_BRANCH"
          echo "::notice::Dispatched quality.yaml against $TEMP_BRANCH via workflow_dispatch"

      - name: Wait for required status checks
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        timeout-minutes: 20
        # AC #4: poll until all required contexts conclude, 20m hard cap.
        # `--required` filters to only ruleset-required checks (ignores optional
        # runs); `--watch` polls every 10s; `--fail-fast` exits 1 on first
        # failure. Pre-sleep lets the workflow_dispatch from the prior step
        # register its check-runs on the PR's head SHA before we start watching.
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER="${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_number }}"
          sleep 15
          if ! gh pr checks "$PR_NUMBER" --required --watch --fail-fast; then
            echo "::error::PR #$PR_NUMBER has failing required check(s). PR left open for manual inspection. Re-run release.yaml after the underlying defect is fixed on main."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "::notice::All required status checks passed on PR #$PR_NUMBER"

      - name: Wait for PR approval or admin-bypass merge
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        id: wait_approval
        # AC #5 (robust): poll until EITHER reviewDecision == APPROVED (the bot
        # PR was approved, auto-merge will fire next) OR merged == true (the
        # maintainer bypass-merged via admin). github-actions[bot] cannot
        # self-approve (universal GitHub rule); `armelhbobdad` is the required
        # approver per the `release` env's required_reviewers list.
        #
        # The merged-true branch handles the scenario where the maintainer
        # chooses to bypass-merge via admin instead of approving via UI (same
        # pattern used on PRs #195/#196/#197 in this repo, where ruleset
        # 13855503's bypass_mode: pull_request admits admin merges without a
        # formal review). In that case, `reviewDecision` stays REVIEW_REQUIRED
        # forever and a pure APPROVED-only poll would hang for 30m.
        #
        # Manual timer (not step-level timeout-minutes) so we can emit a
        # specific ::error:: message on timeout. Exports already_merged so the
        # next two steps skip their work when the maintainer pre-merged.
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER="${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_number }}"
          TIMEOUT_SECONDS=1800  # 30 min
          POLL_INTERVAL=60
          ELAPSED=0
          while [ $ELAPSED -lt $TIMEOUT_SECONDS ]; do
            VIEW=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json reviewDecision,merged)
            DECISION=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r .reviewDecision)
            MERGED=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r .merged)
            if [ "$MERGED" = "true" ]; then
              echo "::notice::PR #$PR_NUMBER already merged (admin-bypass path); skipping auto-merge step."
              echo "already_merged=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
              exit 0
            fi
            if [ "$DECISION" = "APPROVED" ]; then
              echo "::notice::PR #$PR_NUMBER approved; proceeding to auto-merge."
              echo "already_merged=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
              exit 0
            fi
            echo "Waiting (reviewDecision: ${DECISION:-none}; merged: $MERGED; elapsed ${ELAPSED}s/${TIMEOUT_SECONDS}s)"
            sleep $POLL_INTERVAL
            ELAPSED=$((ELAPSED + POLL_INTERVAL))
          done
          echo "::error::PR #$PR_NUMBER was neither approved nor admin-bypass-merged within 30m. PR left open. Re-run release.yaml after approving or merging."
          exit 1

      - name: Auto-merge bot PR
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && steps.wait_approval.outputs.already_merged != 'true'
        # AC #7: --merge (merge-commit), NOT --squash / --rebase. Merge-commit
        # preserves the bot's release commit SHA underneath main's new tip so
        # AC #8's tag anchor on `origin/main` resolves to a reachable commit
        # (the merge commit itself — see the tag step below). --auto blocks
        # nothing because checks + approval are already green.
        # Skipped if the maintainer bypass-merged at the approval step.
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER="${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_number }}"
          if ! gh pr merge "$PR_NUMBER" --auto --merge; then
            echo "::error::Auto-merge failed for PR #$PR_NUMBER. If the error was 'auto merge is not allowed for this repository', run: gh api --method PATCH /repos/${{ github.repository }} -f allow_auto_merge=true  (see Story 3.4 AC #6 / Task 2)."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "::notice::Auto-merge enabled on PR #$PR_NUMBER"

      - name: Wait for merge completion
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && steps.wait_approval.outputs.already_merged != 'true'
        timeout-minutes: 5
        # `gh pr merge --auto` schedules the merge; GitHub may take seconds to
        # actually perform it. Poll `merged` until true so the downstream tag
        # step sees the new main tip. Skipped if the maintainer bypass-merged
        # (merged is already true).
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER="${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_number }}"
          while true; do
            MERGED=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json merged --jq .merged)
            if [ "$MERGED" = "true" ]; then
              echo "::notice::PR #$PR_NUMBER merged"
              break
            fi
            echo "Waiting for PR merge to complete (merged: $MERGED)"
            sleep 10
          done

      - name: Skip PR flow (non-main dispatch ref)
        if: github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'
        # AC #9: feature-branch prereleases preserve the legacy tag-only pattern.
        # The tag will anchor on the CI-ephemeral commit below (orphan; NFR12-
        # compliant per the Story 3.2 alpha-cut precedent at v0.10.1-alpha.0).
        run: |
          echo "::notice::Dispatched from ${{ github.ref }} (not main); skipping PR auto-merge flow."
          echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} will anchor on the CI-ephemeral commit; main was NOT advanced."
          echo "This is the expected path for prerelease cuts from feature branches (Story 3.2 alpha-cut pattern)."

      - name: Create and push tag
        # AC #8: tag creation moved to AFTER the PR auto-merges (main-dispatch
        # path). The anchor is `origin/main`'s new tip (the merge commit), so
        # `git describe --tags` from main resolves cleanly. For non-main dispatch
        # (AC #9), anchor on HEAD (the CI-ephemeral release commit — unchanged
        # from pre-3.4 behavior).
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.ref }}" = "refs/heads/main" ]; then
            git fetch origin main
            TAG_ANCHOR=$(git rev-parse origin/main)
            echo "Main-dispatch: anchoring tag on origin/main tip $TAG_ANCHOR"
          else
            TAG_ANCHOR=HEAD
            echo "Non-main-dispatch: anchoring tag on CI HEAD (orphan pattern per AC #9)"
          fi
          if git rev-parse "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} already exists, skipping tag creation"
          else
            git tag -a "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" "$TAG_ANCHOR"
            git push origin "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          fi

      - name: Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          if   [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then npm publish --tag alpha
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"*  ]]; then npm publish --tag beta
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"rc"*    ]]; then npm publish --tag rc
          else                                     npm publish --tag latest
          fi

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}
          name: "Skill Forge (SKF) v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          body: ${{ steps.release_notes.outputs.RELEASE_NOTES }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'alpha') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'beta') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'rc') }}

      - name: Summary
        # AC #15: extend the step summary with a "Release Flow" subsection so the
        # operator sees at a glance whether the main-dispatch PR-auto-merge path
        # or the non-main-dispatch tag-only path ran.
        run: |
          {
            echo "## 🎉 Released v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo
            echo "**npm**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge/v/${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**GitHub**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/releases/tag/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**Provenance**: auto-attached via OIDC trusted publishing"
            echo
            echo "### Release Flow"
            echo
            if [ "${{ github.ref }}" = "refs/heads/main" ]; then
              echo "- Dispatch ref: \`main\` (Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge path)"
              echo "- Temp branch: \`${{ steps.temp_push.outputs.temp_branch }}\`"
              echo "- Bot PR: ${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_url }}"
              echo "- Required checks: passed (7 contexts)"
              echo "- PR approved + auto-merged via \`--merge\`"
              echo "- Tag anchored on \`origin/main\` tip post-merge"
            else
              echo "- Dispatch ref: \`${{ github.ref }}\` (non-main; tag-only path per AC #9)"
              echo "- Tag anchored on CI-ephemeral commit (orphan pattern)"
            fi
          } >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

- name: Wait for required status checks

content.text: **P5** — Check-run count assertion before `--watch`:

input.old_string:
run: |
          PR_NUMBER="${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_number }}"
          sleep 15
          if ! gh pr checks "$PR_NUMBER" --required --watch --fail-fast; then
            echo "::error::PR #$PR_NUMBER has failing required check(s). PR left open for manual inspection. Re-run release.yaml after the underlying defect is fixed on main."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "::notice::All required status checks passed on PR #$PR_NUMBER"
input.new_string:
run: |
          PR_NUMBER="${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_number }}"
          # P5: wait for at least one required check-run to register before --watch.
          # Without this, `gh pr checks --required --watch --fail-fast` may exit 0
          # immediately if zero required check-runs exist yet (silent-pass bug).
          # The workflow_dispatch from the prior step needs a few seconds to register
          # check-runs on the PR head SHA; poll for up to 2m (24 * 5s) before giving up.
          POLL=0
          REQUIRED_COUNT=0
          while [ $POLL -lt 24 ]; do
            REQUIRED_COUNT=$(gh pr checks "$PR_NUMBER" --required --json name --jq 'length' 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
            if [ "${REQUIRED_COUNT:-0}" -ge 1 ]; then
              echo "::notice::$REQUIRED_COUNT required check-run(s) registered on PR #$PR_NUMBER; entering --watch"
              break
            fi
            sleep 5
            POLL=$((POLL + 1))
          done
          if [ "${REQUIRED_COUNT:-0}" -lt 1 ]; then
            echo "::error::No required check-runs registered on PR #$PR_NUMBER within 2m of workflow_dispatch. Likely causes: (a) quality.yaml didn't actually dispatch (see prior step logs), (b) check-run names don't match the ruleset's required contexts (AC #3 path-3.i caveat — dispatched names may be namespaced differently). PR left open for manual inspection."
            exit 1
          fi
          if ! gh pr checks "$PR_NUMBER" --required --watch --fail-fast; then
            echo "::error::PR #$PR_NUMBER has failing required check(s). PR left open for manual inspection. Re-run release.yaml after the underlying defect is fixed on main."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "::notice::All required status checks passed on PR #$PR_NUMBER"

content.text: **P6 + P7 + P11** — Wait for approval (transient-failure guard, CHANGES_REQUESTED, approver/merge-SHA capture):

input.old_string:
while [ $ELAPSED -lt $TIMEOUT_SECONDS ]; do
            VIEW=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json reviewDecision,merged)
            DECISION=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r .reviewDecision)
            MERGED=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r .merged)
            if [ "$MERGED" = "true" ]; then
              echo "::notice::PR #$PR_NUMBER already merged (admin-bypass path); skipping auto-merge step."
              echo "already_merged=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
              exit 0
            fi
            if [ "$DECISION" = "APPROVED" ]; then
              echo "::notice::PR #$PR_NUMBER approved; proceeding to auto-merge."
              echo "already_merged=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
              exit 0
            fi
            echo "Waiting (reviewDecision: ${DECISION:-none}; merged: $MERGED; elapsed ${ELAPSED}s/${TIMEOUT_SECONDS}s)"
            sleep $POLL_INTERVAL
            ELAPSED=$((ELAPSED + POLL_INTERVAL))
          done
          echo "::error::PR #$PR_NUMBER was neither approved nor admin-bypass-merged within 30m. PR left open. Re-run release.yaml after approving or merging."
          exit 1
input.new_string:
while [ $ELAPSED -lt $TIMEOUT_SECONDS ]; do
            # P6: guard against transient gh / API failures — empty or error VIEW
            # would yield empty DECISION / MERGED and wedge the loop in "none" state
            # while the 30m budget still burns.
            if ! VIEW=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json reviewDecision,merged,mergeCommit,mergedAt,latestReviews 2>&1); then
              echo "gh pr view failed (elapsed ${ELAPSED}s); retrying: $VIEW"
              sleep $POLL_INTERVAL
              ELAPSED=$((ELAPSED + POLL_INTERVAL))
              continue
            fi
            if [ -z "$VIEW" ]; then
              echo "gh pr view returned empty (elapsed ${ELAPSED}s); retrying"
              sleep $POLL_INTERVAL
              ELAPSED=$((ELAPSED + POLL_INTERVAL))
              continue
            fi
            DECISION=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r .reviewDecision)
            MERGED=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r .merged)
            if [ "$MERGED" = "true" ]; then
              # P11: capture merge artifacts for the Summary step (admin-bypass path
              # skips Wait-for-merge, so merge_sha + merged_at must be emitted here).
              MERGE_SHA=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r '.mergeCommit.oid // "unknown"')
              MERGED_AT=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r '.mergedAt // "unknown"')
              echo "::notice::PR #$PR_NUMBER already merged (admin-bypass path) at $MERGED_AT (merge commit $MERGE_SHA); skipping auto-merge step."
              {
                echo "already_merged=true"
                echo "authorization_path=admin-bypass-merge"
                echo "approver=n/a (admin bypass)"
                echo "approved_at=$MERGED_AT"
                echo "merge_sha=$MERGE_SHA"
                echo "merged_at=$MERGED_AT"
              } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
              exit 0
            fi
            # P7: reviewer actively rejected — fail fast, don't burn the 30m budget
            # polling for an APPROVED that won't arrive until the comments are addressed.
            if [ "$DECISION" = "CHANGES_REQUESTED" ]; then
              echo "::error::PR #$PR_NUMBER has CHANGES_REQUESTED; a reviewer actively rejected the PR. Address the review comments and re-dispatch release.yaml."
              exit 1
            fi
            if [ "$DECISION" = "APPROVED" ]; then
              # P11: capture approver identity + timestamp for the Summary step.
              APPROVER=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r '[.latestReviews[]? | select(.state=="APPROVED")] | last | .author.login // "unknown"')
              APPROVED_AT=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r '[.latestReviews[]? | select(.state=="APPROVED")] | last | .submittedAt // "unknown"')
              echo "::notice::PR #$PR_NUMBER approved by @$APPROVER at $APPROVED_AT; proceeding to auto-merge."
              {
                echo "already_merged=false"
                echo "authorization_path=formal-approval"
                echo "approver=$APPROVER"
                echo "approved_at=$APPROVED_AT"
              } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
              exit 0
            fi
            echo "Waiting (reviewDecision: ${DECISION:-none}; merged: $MERGED; elapsed ${ELAPSED}s/${TIMEOUT_SECONDS}s)"
            sleep $POLL_INTERVAL
            ELAPSED=$((ELAPSED + POLL_INTERVAL))
          done
          echo "::error::PR #$PR_NUMBER was neither approved nor admin-bypass-merged within 30m. PR left open. Re-run release.yaml after approving or merging."
          exit 1

content.text: **P6 + P8 + P11** — Wait for merge completion (id, transient-failure guard, fast-exit on closed-not-merged, merge-SHA capture):

input.old_string:
- name: Wait for merge completion
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && steps.wait_approval.outputs.already_merged != 'true'
        timeout-minutes: 5
        # `gh pr merge --auto` schedules the merge; GitHub may take seconds to
        # actually perform it. Poll `merged` until true so the downstream tag
        # step sees the new main tip. Skipped if the maintainer bypass-merged
        # (merged is already true).
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER="${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_number }}"
          while true; do
            MERGED=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json merged --jq .merged)
            if [ "$MERGED" = "true" ]; then
              echo "::notice::PR #$PR_NUMBER merged"
              break
            fi
            echo "Waiting for PR merge to complete (merged: $MERGED)"
            sleep 10
          done
input.new_string:
- name: Wait for merge completion
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && steps.wait_approval.outputs.already_merged != 'true'
        id: wait_merge
        timeout-minutes: 5
        # `gh pr merge --auto` schedules the merge; GitHub may take seconds to
        # actually perform it. Poll `merged` until true so the downstream tag
        # step sees the new main tip. Skipped if the maintainer bypass-merged
        # (merged is already true; wait_approval emitted merge_sha + merged_at
        # for the Summary step).
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER="${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_number }}"
          while true; do
            # P6: guard transient gh / API failures (empty VIEW would hang until
            # the 5m step-level timeout with no diagnostic signal).
            if ! VIEW=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json merged,closed,mergeCommit,mergedAt 2>&1); then
              echo "gh pr view failed; retrying: $VIEW"
              sleep 10
              continue
            fi
            if [ -z "$VIEW" ]; then
              echo "gh pr view returned empty; retrying"
              sleep 10
              continue
            fi
            MERGED=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r .merged)
            CLOSED=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r .closed)
            # P8: PR closed without merge means auto-merge will never fire (operator
            # closed it, GitHub blocked it, mergeable state went bad). Fail fast
            # instead of hanging until the 5m timeout with no actionable diagnostic.
            if [ "$CLOSED" = "true" ] && [ "$MERGED" != "true" ]; then
              echo "::error::PR #$PR_NUMBER was closed without being merged. Investigate: operator close, mergeable state regression, ruleset reject. PR left in closed state."
              exit 1
            fi
            if [ "$MERGED" = "true" ]; then
              # P11: capture merge artifacts for the Summary step.
              MERGE_SHA=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r '.mergeCommit.oid // "unknown"')
              MERGED_AT=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r '.mergedAt // "unknown"')
              echo "::notice::PR #$PR_NUMBER merged at $MERGED_AT (merge commit $MERGE_SHA)"
              {
                echo "merge_sha=$MERGE_SHA"
                echo "merged_at=$MERGED_AT"
              } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
              break
            fi
            echo "Waiting for PR merge to complete (merged: $MERGED; closed: $CLOSED)"
            sleep 10
          done

              MERGE_SHA=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r '.mergeCommit.oid // "unknown"')
              MERGED_AT=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r '.mergedAt // "unknown"')
              echo "::notice::PR #$PR_NUMBER merged at $MERGED_AT (merge commit $MERGE_SHA)"
              {
                echo "merge_sha=$MERGE_SHA"
                echo "merged_at=$MERGED_AT"
              } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
              break
            fi
            echo "Waiting for PR merge to complete (merged: $MERGED; closed: $CLOSED)"
            sleep 10
          done

lines: # `gh pr merge --auto` schedules the merge; GitHub may take seconds to
# actually perform it. Poll `merged` until true so the downstream tag
# step sees the new main tip. Skipped if the maintainer bypass-merged
+        # (merged is already true; wait_approval emitted merge_sha + merged_at
+            # P6: guard transient gh / API failures (empty VIEW would hang until
+            # the 5m step-level timeout with no diagnostic signal).
+            if ! VIEW=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json merged,closed,mergeCommit,mergedAt 2>&1); then
+              echo "gh pr view returned empty; retrying"
+            # P8: PR closed without merge means auto-merge will never fire (operator
+            # closed it, GitHub blocked it, mergeable state went bad). Fail fast
+            # instead of hanging until the 5m timeout with no actionable diagnostic.
+              echo "::error::PR #$PR_NUMBER was closed without being merged. Investigate: operator close, mergeable state regression, ruleset reject. PR left in closed state."
+              # P11: capture merge artifacts for the Summary step.
-            echo "Waiting for PR merge to complete (merged: $MERGED)"
+            echo "Waiting for PR merge to complete (merged: $MERGED; closed: $CLOSED)"

content.text: **P9** — Tag step git-fetch failure + anchor cross-check:

input.old_string:
if [ "${{ github.ref }}" = "refs/heads/main" ]; then
            git fetch origin main
            TAG_ANCHOR=$(git rev-parse origin/main)
            echo "Main-dispatch: anchoring tag on origin/main tip $TAG_ANCHOR"
          else
            TAG_ANCHOR=HEAD
            echo "Non-main-dispatch: anchoring tag on CI HEAD (orphan pattern per AC #9)"
          fi
input.new_string:
if [ "${{ github.ref }}" = "refs/heads/main" ]; then
            # P9: fail fast on fetch failure; cross-check the anchor SHA against the
            # PR's merge commit. git-backend replication can lag the API briefly, so
            # `origin/main` post-fetch may still be the pre-merge tip — in which case
            # the tag would anchor on the orphaned temp-branch commit instead of the
            # merge commit now on main. Using the PR's reported mergeCommit as the
            # authoritative anchor eliminates the race.
            if ! git fetch origin main; then
              echo "::error::git fetch origin main failed. Cannot safely anchor release tag. Check network/auth and re-run."
              exit 1
            fi
            TAG_ANCHOR=$(git rev-parse origin/main)
            PR_NUMBER="${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_number }}"
            EXPECTED_SHA=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json mergeCommit --jq '.mergeCommit.oid // empty')
            if [ -n "$EXPECTED_SHA" ] && [ "$TAG_ANCHOR" != "$EXPECTED_SHA" ]; then
              echo "::warning::origin/main tip ($TAG_ANCHOR) differs from PR #$PR_NUMBER merge commit ($EXPECTED_SHA) — possible git/API replication lag. Using PR merge commit as tag anchor."
              TAG_ANCHOR="$EXPECTED_SHA"
            fi
            echo "Main-dispatch: anchoring tag on $TAG_ANCHOR"
          else
            TAG_ANCHOR=HEAD
            echo "Non-main-dispatch: anchoring tag on CI HEAD (orphan pattern per AC #9)"
          fi

input.new_string:
if [ "${{ github.ref }}" = "refs/heads/main" ]; then
              # P11: richer summary per spec AC #15 — include authorization path,
              # approver identity + timestamp, merge commit SHA + timestamp. Data
              # source: wait_approval emits merge_sha + merged_at on the admin-bypass
              # path (where wait_merge is skipped); wait_merge emits on the formal-
              # approval path. Read from whichever populated.
              AUTH_PATH="${{ steps.wait_approval.outputs.authorization_path }}"
              APPROVER="${{ steps.wait_approval.outputs.approver }}"
              APPROVED_AT="${{ steps.wait_approval.outputs.approved_at }}"
              MERGE_SHA="${{ steps.wait_approval.outputs.merge_sha }}"
              MERGED_AT="${{ steps.wait_approval.outputs.merged_at }}"
              if [ -z "$MERGE_SHA" ]; then
                MERGE_SHA="${{ steps.wait_merge.outputs.merge_sha }}"
                MERGED_AT="${{ steps.wait_merge.outputs.merged_at }}"
              fi
              echo "- Dispatch ref: \`main\` (Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge path)"
              echo "- Temp branch: \`${{ steps.temp_push.outputs.temp_branch }}\`"
              echo "- Bot PR: ${{ steps.open_pr.outputs.pr_url }}"
              echo "- Required checks: passed"
              echo "- Authorization: $AUTH_PATH (approver: \`$APPROVER\` at $APPROVED_AT)"
              echo "- Merged: $MERGED_AT (merge commit \`$MERGE_SHA\`)"
              echo "- Tag anchored on merge commit"
            else
              echo "- Dispatch ref: \`${{ github.ref }}\` (non-main; tag-only path per AC #9)"
              echo "- Tag anchored on CI-ephemeral commit (orphan pattern)"
            fi

content.content:
614	#### Story 3.4 refactor note — PR-auto-merge flow
615	
616	After Story 3.4 (GitHub issue [#198](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/issues/198), PR TBD), `release.yaml` no longer pushes the `release: bump to vX.Y.Z` commit directly to `main`. Instead, a main-dispatched cut pushes the release commit to a temp branch `release/bot/vX.Y.Z-<run_id>`, opens a bot PR against `main`, force-triggers `quality.yaml` against the temp branch via `workflow_dispatch` (so the 7 required status checks run and gate the merge), then auto-merges once checks pass and a maintainer approves. Approval is required at **two** gates — the `release` environment gate at job start, and the PR review-decision gate before auto-merge. Non-main dispatches (feature-branch alpha cuts) skip the PR dance and keep the legacy tag-only behavior. Story 5.2's dev agent on resume will refresh the § Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1 prose above to describe the refactored flow directly.
617

- [ ] [Review][Patch] Temp branch `release/bot/vX.Y.Z-<run_id>` never deleted after merge — add `--delete-branch` to `gh pr merge` [`release.yaml` Auto-merge bot PR, diff 202]
- [ ] [Review][Patch] `release_notes.md` absence produces malformed PR body silently — add `[ -s release_notes.md ]` guard [`release.yaml` Open bot PR, diff 86]
- [ ] [Review][Patch] `gh workflow run quality.yaml` failure silently no-ops; wait-for-checks then hangs — wrap in `|| { echo "::error::..."; exit 1; }` [`release.yaml` Force-trigger, diff 125]
- [ ] [Review][Patch] Silent-pass bug: `gh pr checks --required --watch --fail-fast` may exit 0 when zero required check-runs are registered yet — assert `count>=1` before `--watch` [`release.yaml` Wait for required status checks, diff 136-142]
- [ ] [Review][Patch] `gh pr view` transient API failures yield empty `VIEW` → empty DECISION/MERGED → loop burns timer — guard `[ -z "$VIEW" ]` [`release.yaml` Wait approval 171-188 + Wait merge 216-226]
- [ ] [Review][Patch] `CHANGES_REQUESTED` review decision is not handled; poll runs full 30m after active rejection — exit 1 on `CHANGES_REQUESTED` [`release.yaml` Wait approval, diff 180]
- [ ] [Review][Patch] PR closed (not merged) during wait-for-merge loops until 5m timeout — check `closed && !merged`, exit fast [`release.yaml` Wait for merge completion, diff 217-226]
- [ ] [Review][Patch] `git fetch origin main` failure silently proceeds; API/git-backend replication lag may make `rev-parse origin/main` return stale tip — add `git fetch || exit 1` + cross-check anchor SHA against `gh pr view --json mergeCommit` [`release.yaml` Create and push tag, diff 246-250]
- [ ] [Review][Patch] `docs/RELEASING.md` line 305: "GitHub issue #198, PR TBD" — replace `TBD` with #199 [`docs/RELEASING.md` diff 305]

input.new_string:
#### Patch — all applied 2026-04-23 (local; not yet committed)

- [x] [Review][Patch] P1: `gh pr create` multi-line output may break `PR_URL` parsing — fixed via `| tail -n1` [`release.yaml` Open bot PR]
- [x] [Review][Patch] P2: Temp branch `release/bot/vX.Y.Z-<run_id>` never deleted after merge — fixed via `--delete-branch` on `gh pr merge` [`release.yaml` Auto-merge bot PR]
- [x] [Review][Patch] P3: `release_notes.md` absence produces malformed PR body silently — fixed via `[ ! -s release_notes.md ]` guard + placeholder [`release.yaml` Open bot PR]
- [x] [Review][Patch] P4: `gh workflow run quality.yaml` failure silently no-ops — fixed via `if ! ...; then ::error::; exit 1; fi` wrap [`release.yaml` Force-trigger]
- [x] [Review][Patch] P5: Silent-pass bug: `gh pr checks --required --watch --fail-fast` may exit 0 with zero registered check-runs — fixed via 2-minute count-poll asserting `REQUIRED_COUNT>=1` before `--watch` [`release.yaml` Wait for required status checks]
- [x] [Review][Patch] P6: `gh pr view` transient API failures yield empty `VIEW` → loop burns timer — fixed via exit-code capture + `[ -z "$VIEW" ]` guard with continue; applied to both polling loops [`release.yaml` Wait approval + Wait merge]
- [x] [Review][Patch] P7: `CHANGES_REQUESTED` review decision not handled — fixed via explicit `[ "$DECISION" = "CHANGES_REQUESTED" ] && exit 1` [`release.yaml` Wait approval]
- [x] [Review][Patch] P8: PR closed (not merged) during wait-for-merge loops until 5m timeout — fixed via `closed && !merged` fast-exit [`release.yaml` Wait for merge completion]
- [x] [Review][Patch] P9: `git fetch origin main` failure silently proceeds; replication-lag tag-anchor race — fixed via `git fetch || exit 1` + cross-check `TAG_ANCHOR` against `gh pr view --json mergeCommit` with warning + override [`release.yaml` Create and push tag]
- [x] [Review][Patch] P10: `docs/RELEASING.md` "PR TBD" — replaced with PR #199 link [`docs/RELEASING.md:616`]
- [x] [Review][Patch] P11: AC #15 Summary incomplete — extended with `authorization_path`, approver login, approved_at timestamp, merged_at timestamp, merge commit SHA; data sourced from new step outputs on Wait approval (admin-bypass path) or Wait merge (formal-approval path) [`release.yaml` Wait approval + Wait merge + Summary]

- [x] [Review][Patch] P7: `CHANGES_REQUESTED` review decision not handled — fixed via explicit `[ "$DECISION" = "CHANGES_REQUESTED" ] && exit 1` [`release.yaml` Wait approval]
- [x] [Review][Patch] P8: PR closed (not merged) during wait-for-merge loops until 5m timeout — fixed via `closed && !merged` fast-exit [`release.yaml` Wait for merge completion]
- [x] [Review][Patch] P9: `git fetch origin main` failure silently proceeds; replication-lag tag-anchor race — fixed via `git fetch || exit 1` + cross-check `TAG_ANCHOR` against `gh pr view --json mergeCommit` with warning + override [`release.yaml` Create and push tag]
- [x] [Review][Patch] P10: `docs/RELEASING.md` "PR TBD" — replaced with PR #199 link [`docs/RELEASING.md:616`]
- [x] [Review][Patch] P11: AC #15 Summary incomplete — extended with `authorization_path`, approver login, approved_at timestamp, merged_at timestamp, merge commit SHA; data sourced from new step outputs on Wait approval (admin-bypass path) or Wait merge (formal-approval path) [`release.yaml` Wait approval + Wait merge + Summary]

Hardening assertion (non-empty check list before `--watch`) is tracked as patch P5 in the Review Findings below.

- [x] [Review][Decision] **Force-trigger path choice under check-name mismatch is deferred to live validation** — RESOLVED 2026-04-23: pending-validation risk accepted; Dev Agent Record § "AC #3 path-3.i empirical verification — DEFERRED" is authoritative. content.text:
## ✅ Code review actions complete

- **Decision-needed resolved:** 3 (all amended in the spec)
  - D1 AC #5 amended to sanction both formal-approval (5.a) and admin-bypass (5.b) paths
  - D2 AC #4 amended to sanction `gh pr checks --required` delegation
  - D3 Force-trigger path-3.i risk: pending-validation accepted; Story 5.2 RC cut validates
- **Patches handled:** 11 (all applied locally to `.github/workflows/release.yaml` and `docs/RELEASING.md`)
- **Deferred:** 5 (appended to `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md`)
- **Dismissed:** 14

Workflow YAML validated. Diff stats: `release.yaml` +152/-17, `docs/RELEASING.md` +1/-1.

---

## Story status + sprint sync

Story 3.4's acceptance criteria are now satisfied by the PR #199 code + 11 local patches. However:

- Task 7 (AC #11 live validation) is still `[ ]` unchecked — gates on Story 5.2's RC cut
- The 11 patches are **local-only**, not yet on `main`
- The repo's own refactored flow (PR auto-merge) would be the natural vehicle to land these patches — but Story 5.2 is currently blocked on the pre-patch `release.yaml`, so there's a bootstrapping question

Proposed status: **`in-progress`** (not `done`). Rationale: the review patches need a commit/PR cycle before Story 3.4 is truly shipped, and AC #11 live validation is still pending on Story 5.2 resumption. Setting to `done` now would understate remaining work.

Updating the story Status field and `sprint-status.yaml`.

---

## Review Complete!

**Story Status:** `in-progress` (was `review`)
**Issues Fixed:** 11 (local, uncommitted)
**Action Items Resolved:** 3 decision-needed (spec amendments)
**Deferred:** 5 (tracked in `deferred-work.md`)
**Dismissed:** 14

Local state:
- `.github/workflows/release.yaml` — 11 patches applied (+152/-17), YAML validated
- `docs/RELEASING.md` — PR link fixed
- Story file — ACs #4 and #5 amended; Review Findings section added with all 11 patches checked off
- `deferred-work.md` — 5 new deferred items appended
- `sprint-status.yaml` — 3-4 moved `review → in-progress`

**Remaining work on Story 3.4:**
1. Commit the 11 patches + spec amendments + deferred-work + sprint-status changes
2. Land them on `main` — which requires the refactored workflow OR admin-bypass on a manual PR (the patches themselves don't need to go through `release.yaml`)
3. Story 5.2 resumption will validate AC #11 on the patched code

---

## What would you like to do next?

1. **Commit & open PR with the patches** — I'll stage, commit, and push a PR titled something like `fix(release): harden Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge flow (code-review patches)`
2. **Start the next story** — `/bmad-dev-story` to pick up the next `ready-for-dev` story (Story 5.2 is currently `in-progress` and blocked pending these patches)
3. **Done** — end the review workflow; you'll commit the patches yourself
4. **Re-run code review** — address something I missed and review again

Reply with the number.

content.content: Switched to a new branch 'fix/release-pr-auto-merge-hardening'

input.command:
git add .github/workflows/release.yaml docs/RELEASING.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(release): harden Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge flow

Eleven defensive patches surfaced by /bmad-code-review Task 9 on PR #199.
Hardens the PR-auto-merge release flow against transient failures, silent
no-ops, and diagnostic gaps. No behavioral change on the happy path.

Patches:

- P1: gh pr create stdout may contain warning/notice lines before URL —
  capture with `| tail -n1` to pin to the URL line.
- P2: --delete-branch on `gh pr merge --auto --merge` cleans up
  release/bot/vX.Y.Z-<run_id> refs instead of accumulating one per release.
- P3: `release_notes.md` missing/empty now yields a placeholder body with
  a ::warning:: instead of a silently malformed PR body.
- P4: `gh workflow run quality.yaml` failure is now surfaced explicitly;
  previously a silent no-op let wait-for-checks hang until 20m timeout.
- P5: Silent-pass bug fix — `gh pr checks --required --watch --fail-fast`
  could exit 0 when zero required check-runs were registered yet. New
  pre-watch poll (up to 2m) asserts REQUIRED_COUNT>=1 before entering
  --watch; catches the AC #3 path-3.i check-name-mismatch failure mode
  with a specific, actionable error instead of a generic 20m timeout.
- P6: Transient `gh pr view` failures in wait-approval and wait-merge
  polling loops now capture exit code + guard empty output and retry,
  instead of burning the timer budget in "none" state.
- P7: CHANGES_REQUESTED review decision now fails fast with a specific
  error, instead of polling the full 30m for an APPROVED that can't
  arrive until review comments are addressed.
- P8: PR closed (not merged) during wait-for-merge now fast-exits with
  a specific error, instead of hanging until 5m timeout with no signal.
- P9: `git fetch origin main` failure now fails fast; the tag anchor SHA
  is cross-checked against `gh pr view --json mergeCommit` to catch git/
  API replication lag (would otherwise anchor tag on pre-merge origin
  tip, orphaning the release commit).
- P10: docs/RELEASING.md "PR TBD" replaced with PR #199 link.
- P11: Summary step extended per AC #15 to include authorization_path
  (formal-approval vs admin-bypass-merge), approver login, approved_at
  timestamp, merged_at timestamp, and merge commit SHA. New step outputs
  on Wait-approval (admin-bypass path, wait_merge skipped) and Wait-merge
  (formal-approval path) feed the Summary.

Also amends Story 3.4 spec (local, gitignored):
- AC #4 sanctions `gh pr checks --required` delegation over the hard-coded
  check-name bash array (single source of truth; eliminates drift class).
- AC #5 sanctions both formal-approval (5.a) and admin-bypass-merge (5.b)
  paths explicitly. Commit c9ccffc's behavior is now spec-compliant.

Five edge cases deferred to follow-up hardening (see deferred-work.md):
admin-bypass-BEFORE-checks race, --fail-fast on flaky tests, tag-already-
exists retry skips remote push, manual `gh run cancel` mid-merge, and the
remaining AC #16 defense-in-depth handlers for failure classes (a/c/g/h).

AC #11 live validation still pends on Story 5.2's v1.0.0-rc.1 RC cut.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1

input.command:
gh pr create --title "fix(release): harden Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge flow" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Eleven defensive patches surfaced by `/bmad-code-review` Task 9 on [PR #199](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/pull/199). Hardens the Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge release flow against transient failures, silent no-ops, and diagnostic gaps. No behavioral change on the happy path; failure modes now produce specific actionable errors instead of generic timeouts.

- **P1–P4** Input-validation and error-surfacing patches: multi-line `gh pr create` stdout handling, `--delete-branch` cleanup, missing `release_notes.md` guard, `gh workflow run` failure surfacing
- **P5** Fixes a **silent-pass bug** where `gh pr checks --required --watch --fail-fast` could exit 0 with zero required check-runs registered — now asserts `REQUIRED_COUNT>=1` before `--watch` with a specific error on AC #3 path-3.i check-name mismatch
- **P6–P8** Polling-loop resilience: transient `gh pr view` failure guards, `CHANGES_REQUESTED` fast-exit, PR-closed-not-merged fast-exit
- **P9** Tag anchor cross-checked against `gh pr view --json mergeCommit` to defend against git/API replication lag that would otherwise orphan the release commit
- **P10** `docs/RELEASING.md` "PR TBD" → [PR #199](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/pull/199)
- **P11** AC #15 Summary extended with `authorization_path`, approver login, approved_at/merged_at timestamps, and merge commit SHA

Five lower-priority edge cases tracked for follow-up hardening (admin-bypass-BEFORE-checks race, `--fail-fast` on flaky tests, tag-already-exists retry, manual `gh run cancel` mid-merge, remaining AC #16 defense-in-depth handlers).

Story 3.4 spec local amendments: AC #4 sanctions `gh pr checks --required` delegation (single source of truth); AC #5 sanctions both formal-approval (5.a) and admin-bypass-merge (5.b) paths (aligns spec with `c9ccffc` behavior).

AC #11 live validation still pends on Story 5.2's `v1.0.0-rc.1` RC cut — this PR must land on `main` before Story 5.2 resumes.

## Test plan

- [x] Local `npm test` (invoked by pre-commit hook): PASSED
- [x] YAML syntax validated
- [ ] `quality.yaml` required checks pass on this PR (runs automatically)
- [ ] Live validation on Story 5.2's RC cut exercises the hardened flow end-to-end (the patched `release.yaml` is the code under test)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1

content.text:
## PR opened: [#200](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/pull/200)

- **Branch:** `fix/release-pr-auto-merge-hardening` → `main`
- **Commit:** `9467b4d fix(release): harden Story 3.4 PR-auto-merge flow`
- **Files:** `.github/workflows/release.yaml` (+152/-17), `docs/RELEASING.md` (+1/-1)

Once the 7 required checks pass and you approve (or admin-bypass merge), Story 3.4 is fully shipped — then Story 5.2's RC cut validates AC #11 against the patched flow.
